title: setup.sh not executable - SMF will fail to start
description: images/triton-api/boot/setup.sh committed with mode 100644. SMF postboot manifest execs it directly. Fix: git update-index --chmod=+x images/triton-api/boot/setup.sh

title: P0: tag.rs inline tests validate test-only helpers, not production parse_tag_value
description: The test module in cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/tag.rs defines its own parse_tag and parse_tags_from_args helper functions that do NOT perform the bool/number type coercion that the production parse_tag_value function does. The production function converts "true" -> Bool(true), "42" -> Number(42), etc., but the test-only functions store everything as Value::String. This means production type coercion logic is completely untested. Fix: add tests for parse_tag_value directly, or replace test-only helpers with calls to the production code.

title: P0: volume_list.json fixture uses wrong wire format (ownerUuid instead of owner_uuid)
description: The fixture file cli/triton-cli/tests/fixtures/volume_list.json uses camelCase field names (ownerUuid, filesystemPath) but the Volume struct has explicit #[serde(rename = "owner_uuid")] and #[serde(rename = "filesystem_path")] overrides. The fixture cannot actually deserialize into the Volume struct. The corresponding test test_volume_fixture_camel_case_fields in cli_output_format.rs has its assertions backwards (asserts camelCase present and snake_case absent, when it should be the opposite). Evidence: emit-payload-fakes.json correctly uses owner_uuid. Fix both the fixture and the test assertions.

title: Insecure password generation in RBAC apply
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:857-875 — generate_password() uses SystemTime::now().as_nanos() as its sole entropy source, which is trivially predictable. Used for newly created RBAC sub-users. Fix: use uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string() (uses OS entropy) or add getrandom crate.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 3B: triton-cli instance/RBAC commands review. Rated CRITICAL — insecure entropy source for passwords.

title: Package.flexible_disk silently drops data due to serde rename mismatch
description: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/misc.rs:54,91-92 — Package struct has #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] so serde expects flexibleDisk, but CloudAPI sends flexible_disk (snake_case). Since the field has #[serde(default)], it silently becomes None even when the API returns true. Existing tests never assert on this field. Fix: add #[serde(rename = "flexible_disk")] override on the field and add test assertion.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: From review 2B: cloudapi-api types review. Rated CRITICAL — silent data loss.

title: Shell injection in triton env output
description: Profile values (url, account, key_id) are interpolated into shell export statements without escaping in cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs:29-45. When a user runs eval "$(triton env)", a malicious profile value (e.g. from triton profile create -f untrusted.json) could execute arbitrary commands. Affects bash, fish, and PowerShell output modes. Fix: escape shell metacharacters before interpolation.

title: manta-storage S3 surface: SigV4 negative-auth returns HTTP 400 instead of 403
description:
Found by manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-sigv4-auth-test.sh against vnext mantad at 192.168.1.182:7443.

S3 spec: SigV4 authentication failures MUST return HTTP 403 with one of:
  - SignatureDoesNotMatch     (bad signature)
  - InvalidAccessKeyId        (unknown AKID)
  - RequestTimeTooSkewed      (clock drift > 15min)
  - AccessDenied              (general auth fail)
  - AuthorizationHeaderMalformed (missing/bad component)

Mantad currently returns HTTP 400 (BadRequest) for all six negative-auth
test cases. 0/6 pass.

Repro:
  S3_ENDPOINT=http://192.168.1.182:7443 \
    MANTA_USER=root AWS_REGION=us-east-1 \
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=<root-akid> AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=<root-secret> \
    bash manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-sigv4-auth-test.sh

Failing assertions (0/6 pass):
  FAIL Expected 403 for missing date header, got 400
  FAIL Invalid signature should be rejected but wasn't  (i.e. got 400 with wrong code)
  FAIL Invalid access key should be rejected but wasn't
  FAIL Expected 403 for request time too skewed, got 400
  FAIL Expected 403 for general authentication failure, got 400
  FAIL Expected 403 for PUT request with application/x-directory and invalid auth, got 400

Fix shape: in the SigV4 verifier error-mapping boundary
(crates/mantas3/s3/src/auth/* or s3_impl::middleware::auth), map each
SigV4 verifier error variant to the corresponding S3 error code +
HTTP 403, not a generic BadRequest (400). Priority is P1 because
S3 clients (aws-cli, aws-sdk-*) interpret 400-vs-403 differently for
retry logic and observability — a 400 means 'fix your request body',
a 403 means 'fix your creds'.

Reviewer hint: 'missing date header' specifically — that's a request
malformation, not a signature failure. The spec answer is 403
AuthorizationHeaderMalformed, not 400. The whole family of auth-related
errors should land at the same HTTP status.
close_reason: wontfix: mantad's 400 matches real AWS S3 behavior — x-amz-content-sha256 is a mandatory header for SigV4 S3 requests; the test omits it on every probe. When x-amz-content-sha256 is included the auth path returns spec-compliant 403 InvalidAccessKeyId / SignatureDoesNotMatch. Test should be updated upstream.

comments.author: neirac
comments.text: Investigated 2026-06-03 against vnext mantad on 192.168.1.182:7443. ROOT CAUSE: All six failing test cases in s3-sigv4-auth-test.sh send a forged Authorization header but omit x-amz-content-sha256. The upstream s3s crate (v0.13.0, ops/signature.rs:355-357) rejects S3 requests at header validation BEFORE SigV4 verification when x-amz-content-sha256 is absent, returning 400 InvalidRequest. This matches the AWS S3 spec: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/sigv4-auth-using-authorization-header.html "When you use the Authorization header to authenticate a request, you must include this header [x-amz-content-sha256]." VERIFICATION: When a request includes x-amz-content-sha256 and a bogus SigV4, mantad returns the correct S3 errors: $ curl -X GET -H "Authorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=AKIA-UNKNOWN/.../s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=...;x-amz-content-sha256;x-amz-date, Signature=invalid" -H "x-amz-content-sha256: " -H "x-amz-date: ..." ... HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden InvalidAccessKeyIdunknown access key $ curl ... (valid AKID, bogus signature) HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden SignatureDoesNotMatch The SigV4 error mapping in our s3 surface is spec-compliant. The test expectations are inherited from the legacy Node.js manta-buckets-api, which was auth-first / payload-validation-second (and so returned 403 even for requests missing required headers). RESOLUTION: wontfix. Two follow-ups: - Mark s3-sigv4-auth-test.sh as known-incompatible-with-vnext, OR - Update each curl probe to include 'x-amz-content-sha256: UNSIGNED-PAYLOAD' (or the SHA-256 of the body) so the auth path is actually exercised. The tests will then pass on mantad — and on real AWS S3, which currently returns 400 for the same payloads.

title: image.defs.mk: silent failure on git rev-parse
description: shell git rev-parse --show-toplevel failure sets ENGBLD_REPO_ROOT to empty string. All subsequent include paths resolve to /deps/eng/... causing confusing cascading errors. Add ifeq guard after assignment.

title: P1: VMAPI vm_action endpoint uses untyped serde_json::Value body, preventing Progenitor from generating MigrationAction
description: The VMAPI API trait's vm_action endpoint takes TypedBody<serde_json::Value> instead of typed request bodies. This means MigrationAction (and other action types) never appear in the OpenAPI spec, so Progenitor cannot generate them. The CLI must import MigrationAction from the API crate re-export and derive clap::ValueEnum directly on the API type — unlike all other CLI enum args which use Progenitor-generated types with ValueEnum added via client-generator patches. Fix: define typed action request bodies in vmapi-api (matching the cloudapi-api action-dispatch pattern) so MigrationAction appears in the OpenAPI spec and Progenitor generates it.

title: P1: load_config() in test helpers silently swallows file read and JSON parse errors
P1: parse_volume_size u64 overflow on large GiB values
description:
parse_volume_size in cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs multiplies gib * 1024 to convert GiB to MiB, but this can overflow u64 with valid-looking input like '18014398509481984G'. The function accepts user input (volume size argument), so this is a real production risk. No test documents or guards against this.

Fix: Use checked_mul() or saturating_mul() and return an error on overflow. Add a test for the overflow case.
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs (parse_volume_size function)

title: P1: Conditional assertions in cli_manage_workflow.rs silently skip on missing data
P1: Weak UUID validation in test assertions uses contains('-') instead of proper parsing

Five integration test files validate UUIDs with first_id.contains('-'), which matches any string containing a hyphen (e.g., 'not-a-uuid', 'hello-world'). Should use uuid::Uuid::parse_str(first_id).is_ok() or a proper UUID regex. Consider adding a common::assert_uuid() helper.

Files affected:
- cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_images.rs
- cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_networks.rs
- cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_packages.rs

title: P1: 35+ integration tests at Level 0-1 lack output content validation
description:
Multiple test files have tests that only check exit code (.success() or .failure()) without verifying output content:

- cli_api_errors.rs: 14 of 23 tests at Level 1 (exit-code only). The error-handling test file does the least to verify error messages.
- cli_error_paths.rs: 15 of 37 tests at Level 1. The 11 zero-args-succeeds tests don't verify empty stdout/stderr. The 22 required-arg tests only check for generic word 'required' without specifying which argument.
- cli_image_create.rs: 6 *_no_args tests check .failure() only without stderr assertion.

Fix: add .stderr(predicate::str::contains(...)) to failure tests, add .stdout("").stderr("") to zero-args-succeeds tests. Files: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_api_errors.rs, cli_error_paths.rs, cli_image_create.rs

title: P1: ~35 API tests silently pass when config is missing (early return pattern)
description: Every #[ignore] API integration test has a guard pattern like: if !common::config::has_integration_config() { return; } This means when tests are un-ignored for API runs but config is missing/broken, the tests silently report as passed (green) instead of failing. This masks config problems and gives false confidence. Affects all test files with API tests (~35 tests total). Fix: replace bare return with panic!("integration config required") so failures are visible when tests are deliberately enabled. Files: all cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_*.rs files with #[ignore] tests.

title: P1: No image list filter verification tests (--type silently ignored)
description: The image list command supports --type, --state, --os, --name, --version filters, but zero tests verify filtering actually works. This is especially significant because there is a known history of the type filter being silently ignored. No test lists images with --type zone-dataset and verifies the result set is actually filtered. A test should: (1) list all images, (2) list with --type <value>, (3) assert filtered count < total, (4) assert every returned item has the correct type field. Files: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_images.rs, cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs

title: P1: format_age() in output/mod.rs has zero test coverage
description: format_age() is used in every list table's AGE column. It has 6+ branch conditions (years, weeks, days, hours, minutes, seconds) plus error handling and negative-duration paths. Zero unit tests exist for this function. A mutation like swapping hours/minutes labels or returning empty string would go undetected. Location: cli/triton-cli/src/output/mod.rs. Add unit tests covering each time bracket, edge cases (just now, future timestamps), and error paths (invalid timestamp string, empty string).

title: Missing serde rename overrides on CreateMachineRequest fields
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs, CreateMachineRequest struct (line 320, rename_all = "camelCase")

Four fields lack explicit serde rename overrides but the Node.js wire format uses snake_case:

1. firewall_enabled (line 355) - auto-renames to firewallEnabled, wire uses firewall_enabled
2. deletion_protection (line 358) - auto-renames to deletionProtection, wire uses deletion_protection
3. delegate_dataset (line 372) - auto-renames to delegateDataset, wire uses delegate_dataset
4. allow_shared_images (line 378) - auto-renames to allowSharedImages, wire uses allow_shared_images

Evidence: The Machine response struct already has explicit renames for firewall_enabled, deletion_protection,
and delegate_dataset, confirming the Node.js API uses snake_case for these fields.

Critical: CreateMachineRequest has #[serde(flatten)] extra which would silently swallow the snake_case
fields from node-triton into the HashMap rather than raising an error.

Fix: Add #[serde(rename = "...")] with snake_case names to each field:
  #[serde(rename = "firewall_enabled")]
  pub firewall_enabled: Option<bool>,
  #[serde(rename = "deletion_protection")]
  pub deletion_protection: Option<bool>,
  #[serde(rename = "delegate_dataset")]
  pub delegate_dataset: Option<bool>,
  #[serde(rename = "allow_shared_images")]
  pub allow_shared_images: Option<bool>,

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make openapi-check

title: RBAC role edit silently drops malformed PolicyRef/MemberRef entries
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/role.rs lines 515 and 536, serde_json::from_value::<PolicyRef>().ok() and from_value::<MemberRef>().ok() inside filter_map silently discard entries that fail to parse. description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/profile.rs:184-188, if let Ok(profile) = Profile::load(name).await silently drops profiles that fail to load (corrupt JSON, permissions, I/O errors). Users see a shorter list with no warning. Should emit tracing::warn! for each failed load.

title: read_string uses unwrap_or_default() for invalid UTF-8 in SSH agent protocol
description: libs/triton-auth/src/ssh_agent.rs:95 — String::from_utf8(...).unwrap_or_default() silently produces an empty string when the SSH agent returns non-UTF-8 data. This hides protocol errors and causes key type matching to silently fail (empty string won't match 'ssh-rsa' etc). Fix: return an AuthError::AgentError with context about the invalid UTF-8 at the given offset.

title: Debug format produces doubled HTTP/ prefix in cloudapi command
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/cloudapi.rs:162 — println\!("HTTP/{:?} {}", response.version(), response.status()) produces 'HTTP/HTTP/1.1 200 OK' because http::Version's Debug impl already includes the 'HTTP/' prefix. Violates CLAUDE.md Type Safety Rule 7. Fix: remove the 'HTTP/' prefix from the format string: println\!("{:?} {}", response.version(), response.status())

title: load_from_common_paths skips PKCS#1/legacy keys during auto-discovery
description:
## Summary

`KeyLoader::load_from_common_paths()` in `libs/triton-auth/src/key_loader.rs:225-258`
only calls `load_from_file()` for each candidate key in `~/.ssh/`. That method rejects
non-OpenSSH formats (PKCS#1, SEC1, DSA, PKCS#8) with an error. It never falls back to
`load_legacy_from_file()`, which supports all these formats.

This means any user whose `~/.ssh/id_rsa` is in PKCS#1 PEM format
(`-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----`) — which is common for older keys — gets:

    Error: Error: Key not found: No key with fingerprint ... found in ~/.ssh/

Node.js triton loads these keys successfully.

This is differences.md issue #1 and blocks all API commands for affected users.

## Root cause

`load_from_common_paths()` line 240:
```rust
if let Ok(key) = Self::load_from_file(&path, None).await {
    if fingerprint.matches(key.public_key()) {
        return Ok(key);
    }
}
```

When `load_from_file` returns Err for a PKCS#1 key, the loop silently continues
to the next file. It never tries `load_legacy_from_file` as a fallback.

## Fix

After `load_from_file` fails, try `load_legacy_from_file` before moving on.
The challenge is that `load_from_file` returns `PrivateKey` (ssh_key crate)
while `load_legacy_from_file` returns `LegacyPrivateKey`. The caller
(`load_private_key` via `KeySource::Auto`) would need to handle both, or
`load_from_common_paths` needs a new return type / the two key types need
unification.

Additionally, `load_from_common_paths` only checks 4 hardcoded filenames
(`id_ed25519`, `id_ecdsa`, `id_rsa`, `id_dsa`). Node.js triton scans all
files in `~/.ssh/` by fingerprint. This is a secondary issue but worth
noting.

## Integration test to add

File: `libs/triton-auth/tests/fs_keys_test.rs`

The existing tests all call `load_legacy_from_file` directly. There is no
test for `load_from_common_paths` or the `KeySource::Auto` code path. Add:

1. **Test: PKCS#1 RSA key discovered via `load_from_common_paths`**
   - Create a temp dir as fake `$HOME`
   - Place the existing test PKCS#1 key (`tests/keys/id_rsa`) at `$HOME/.ssh/id_rsa`
   - Compute its fingerprint (known: `fa:56:a1:6b:cc:04:97:fe:e2:98:54:c4:2e:0d:26:c6`)
   - Call `load_from_common_paths` (or `load_private_key` with `KeySource::Auto`)
     with that fingerprint
   - Assert it succeeds and the key type is RSA

2. **Test: ECDSA SEC1 key discovered via `load_from_common_paths`**
   - Same pattern with `tests/keys/id_ecdsa` (fingerprint: `00:74:32:ae:0a:24:3c:7a:e7:07:b8:ee:91:c4:c7:27`)

3. **Test: OpenSSH format key still works** (regression guard)
   - Generate or provide an OpenSSH-format ed25519 key
   - Verify `load_from_common_paths` finds it

Note: `load_from_common_paths` is `async fn` (private). It may need to be
made `pub(crate)` for direct testing, or tests can go through
`load_private_key(KeySource::Auto { .. })` which is the public entry point.
The `Auto` path tries the SSH agent first and falls back to
`load_from_common_paths`, so the test environment should ensure no SSH agent
is running (or `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` is unset).

## Existing test keys available

- `tests/keys/id_rsa` — PKCS#1 PEM format, MD5: `fa:56:a1:6b:cc:04:97:fe:e2:98:54:c4:2e:0d:26:c6`
- `tests/keys/id_ecdsa` — SEC1 PEM format, MD5: `00:74:32:ae:0a:24:3c:7a:e7:07:b8:ee:91:c4:c7:27`

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on ImageState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs, lines 27-33

ImageState enum is deserialized from server responses (used in Image struct at line 143) but lacks a #[serde(other)] catch-all variant. If the server adds a new image state, deserialization will fail.

Current definition:
```rust
pub enum ImageState {
    Active,
    Unactivated,
    Disabled,
    Creating,
    Failed,
}
```

Suggested fix: Add an Unknown catch-all variant:
```rust
pub enum ImageState {
    Active,
    Unactivated,
    Disabled,
    Creating,
    Failed,
    /// Unknown state (forward compatibility)
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```

Note: ImageState also has clap::ValueEnum derived. The Unknown variant will need #[clap(skip)] to prevent it from appearing as a CLI argument option.

Verification: Run `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api` and `make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api` to confirm compilation and tests pass.

title: Error schema patching should use Progenitor with_patch instead of patched/ directory
description:
Files:
- openapi-manager/src/transforms.rs (post-generation transform)
- clients/internal/cloudapi-client/build.rs:14-16 (reads from patched/)
- openapi-specs/patched/cloudapi-api.json (patched spec)

**Problem:** CloudAPI returns errors as `{code, message, request_id?}` but Dropshot generates `{error_code, message, request_id}` (required). Current workaround maintains a separate `openapi-specs/patched/` directory with a post-generation transform in openapi-manager.

**Suggested fix:** Use `with_patch("Error", &error_patch)` in cloudapi-client build.rs to rename `error_code` → `code` and make `request_id` optional, then read from `openapi-specs/generated/` directly. Eliminates the patched/ directory entirely.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-client && make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Keeping both generated/ and patched/ specs is intentional. Generated spec = Dropshot trait (future Rust impl), patched spec = actual Node.js wire format. The diff is valuable migration documentation. Patched dir gets deleted when Rust replaces Node.js.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: Won't fix: keeping both generated/ and patched/ specs is intentional. Generated = Dropshot trait (future Rust impl), patched = actual Node.js wire format. The diff documents the migration gap. Patched dir gets deleted when Rust replaces Node.js.

**Missing:** `#[serde(other)] Unknown` catch-all variant

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on VolumeState
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs, lines 25-30

**Current variants:** Creating, Ready, Failed, Deleting
**Missing:** `#[serde(other)] Unknown` catch-all variant

**Problem:** If CloudAPI adds new volume states in the future, deserialization will fail with an error instead of gracefully falling back. Other state enums (MachineState, SnapshotState, MigrationState, MigrationPhase) correctly use the `#[serde(other)] Unknown` pattern.

**Suggested fix:**
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum VolumeState {
    Creating,
    Ready,
    Failed,
    Deleting,
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make openapi-check`

title: mantas3-s3 PutObject: x-amz-tagging request header ignored (tags not persisted)
description:
Found by manta-ng/bench/s3_suite.py against vnext mantad on
192.168.1.182:7443 (acfh-fixed binary, sha e5a1878).

Failing test: s3_suite.py::t_put_object_with_tagging_header (line 3904).

Test payload:

  h.c.put_object(
      Bucket=h.bucket, Key="inline-tag",
      Body=b"tagged-at-upload",
      Tagging="env=staging&team=storage",
  )
  got = h.c.get_object_tagging(Bucket=h.bucket, Key="inline-tag")
  tags = {t["Key"]: t["Value"] for t in got["TagSet"]}
  assert tags == {"env": "staging", "team": "storage"}

Observed: GetObjectTagging returns {} (empty TagSet).
Expected: tags set via x-amz-tagging header on PutObject persist.

AWS S3 spec: clients can attach tags inline at upload time via the
x-amz-tagging request header on PutObject (form-encoded
"key1=value1&key2=value2&..."). This avoids the round-trip of
PutObject + PutObjectTagging. boto3 surfaces this as the Tagging=
keyword on put_object.

Mantad currently ignores the x-amz-tagging header and stores the
object with tags = []. Already-existing PutObjectTagging path works
correctly (covered by btba + tag_key_too_long / tag_value_too_long
which pass).

FIX SHAPE:
  - In crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/ops/object.rs::put_object_impl,
    parse the x-amz-tagging header (form-urlencoded) when present,
    validate via tags_from_dto(..., MAX_OBJECT_TAGS), and stamp
    onto the ObjectMeta.tags before put_object.
  - The s3s framework surfaces this header as
    s3s::dto::PutObjectInput::tagging: Option<String>.

TEST COVERAGE TARGETS:
  - Round-trip: PutObject with Tagging="env=prod" -> GetObjectTagging
    returns [{"env","prod"}]
  - Validation: invalid tag in header rejected (same boundaries as
    body-based PutObjectTagging)
  - MultipartUpload: x-amz-tagging on CreateMultipartUpload should
    flow to the completed object too (manta-ng covers this in
    t_mpu_per_request_*).

PRIORITY: P2 -- pretty common upload pattern; aws-cli's
`aws s3 cp --metadata tags=...` uses this header.

NOT BLOCKING by acfh; pre-existing gap in PutObject. Surfaced by
running s3_suite.py for the first time against vnext mantad.

comments.author: neirac
comments.text:
Implemented in manta-storage ceb1ff6 on surface-s3.

FIX: put_object_impl now reads input.tagging (s3s::dto::TaggingHeader =
String, the form-urlencoded value) via the new tags_from_header()
helper, then stamps the parsed pairs onto ObjectMeta.tags on both
the Replicated and EC durability branches.

LIVE VERIFY (192.168.1.182:7443, mantad sha 8bdddc4c39e97bac861e52a00df47e2d327a7cdd5e51a8ebcacf6c2c99ba3f1d):

  Test 1: aws s3api put-object --tagging "env=staging&team=storage"
          + get-object-tagging
    -> 200, TagSet round-trips both pairs in order.

  Test 2: aws s3api put-object --tagging "k1=v1&...&k11=v11"
    -> 400 TooManyTags: "too many tags: 11 > max 10"

  Test 3: aws s3api put-object --tagging "name=Alice%20%26%20Bob"
          + get-object-tagging
    -> 200, percent-decoded TagSet returns [{name, "Alice & Bob"}]

UNIT + INTEGRATION TESTS (149/149 pass on build01, +13 over the
pre-tpy0 baseline of 136):
  - 9 new unit tests in s3_impl::shared::tags::tests (header_*)
  - 4 new integration tests in crates/mantas3/s3/tests/x_amz_tagging_header.rs

Closes tpy0.

title: mantas3-s3 gate: scope_authorized() bypasses ACL on any non-empty scope; doesn't validate bucket/level/key_prefix
description:
Discovered while landing monitor-reef-971j (scoped-access-key admin endpoint).

CURRENT BEHAVIOR (mantas3-s3/src/s3_impl/shared/caller.rs:77-94):

When the AccessKey has ANY scope entries, the S3 gate sites short-circuit
the ACL/ownership check across the caller's entire workspace. The actual
contents of the scope list -- bucket name, level, key_prefix -- are NOT
inspected at the gate site.

The data-plane row already has the right shape:

  AccessKey.scope: Vec[ScopeEntry]
  ScopeEntry { bucket, level, key_prefix }

But the gate doesn't consult it. The author was explicit at caller.rs:326-335:

  The S3 surface short-circuit lands in this slice; the access-key-side
  scope field is a follow-up.

971j now ships the admin surface to MINT scoped keys, so the follow-up is
no longer hypothetical: any operator who uses 'tcadm user scoped-key
create' gets a key that bypasses ACL on EVERY bucket in their workspace,
not just the listed buckets.

FIX SHAPE:
  1. Add scope_allows(bucket, key, required_level) on CallerContext.
  2. Map S3 ops to required ScopeLevel:
       Read       : GetObject, HeadObject, ListBucket, HeadBucket
       ReadWrite  : + PutObject, DeleteObject, multipart write ops
       Full       : + PutBucketAcl, PutBucketPolicy, PutPublicAccessBlock,
                     DeleteBucket, lifecycle / cors / versioning
  3. Replace the bare scope_authorized at every gate site with
     scope_allows(bucket, key, level).
  4. Extend tests with bucket-mismatch, level-mismatch, prefix-mismatch
     coverage.

TEST TARGETS:
  - scoped key on bucket A cannot read bucket B (same workspace)
  - scoped key with level=Read on bucket A cannot PutObject
  - scoped key with key_prefix='pub/' allows GET pub/x, denies GET priv/y
  - scoped key with level=Full allows DeleteBucket

PRIORITY: P2 -- security-relevant: closing the IAM-user blast radius
once scoped keys are in operator use.

EVIDENCE: crates/mantas3/s3/tests/scoped_keys_e2e.rs pins the current
"bypass on any scope" contract for regression purposes; the new bead
should flip that contract to per-scope-entry validation.

comments.author: neirac
comments.text:
Implemented in manta-storage 40d3ebf on surface-s3.

Replaced caller.scope_authorized() (coarse: "any scope grants ACL
bypass") with caller.scope_allows(bucket, key?, required_level) at
all 29 gate sites in s3_impl/ops/. The new check inspects the
AccessKey's persisted ScopeEntry list and only fires the bypass when
(bucket, key?, level) is actually granted.

Semantics (manta-ng parity):
  - bucket name match required
  - level coverage: Full > ReadWrite > Read
  - key_prefix narrows per-key reads; bucket-level ops accept any
    prefix (matches AWS s3:ListBucket / s3:prefix interplay)

Per-op level mapping:
  Read       Get/HeadObject, List*, HeadBucket, GetBucketAcl/Location/PAB,
             GetObjectTagging, Get*ObjectRetention/LegalHold, ListParts,
             ListMultipartUploads, copy source side
  ReadWrite  Put/Delete/CopyObject (dest), CreateMultipartUpload,
             UploadPart, CompleteMultipartUpload, AbortMultipartUpload,
             Put/DeleteObjectTagging, bulk DeleteObjects
  Full       Put/DeletePublicAccessBlock, PutObjectRetention,
             PutObjectLegalHold

NEW TESTS (6/6 pass on build01, crates/mantas3/s3/tests/scoped_keys_e2e.rs):
  - scoped_key_denied_on_bucket_not_in_scope    (cross-bucket reject)
  - scoped_key_denied_on_level_mismatch          (Read scope denies PUT)
  - scoped_key_prefix_narrows_object_reads       (pub/x ok, priv/y deny)
  - scoped_key_full_level_subsumes_readwrite     (Full allows PUT)
  + 2 pre-existing scenarios still pass

REGRESSIONS: 136/136 on build01 across mantas3-s3, mantas3-cluster,
mantad-client. +4 over the 132 pre-acfh baseline (the 4 new
scoped-key e2e scenarios).

LIVE VERIFY: stripped release mantad shipped to 192.168.1.182:7443
(sha e5a1878192dec1c32892a357aea1952a89d2ca04a0629ac943364e08dcefc312).
Re-minted a scoped key against the live endpoint:

  POST /admin/v1/users/acfh-alice/scoped-access-keys
    {"scope":[{"bucket":"acfh-alice-bucket","level":"read"}]}
  -> 200 with persisted scope, AKID MKIAN2IKZ56XDC2OAJLH

S3 compat sweep against the acfh-fixed mantad in progress (task
bxcjk132d).

Note: scope_authorized() retained as a coarse predicate for the
existing test fixtures (#[allow(dead_code)] since no gate site uses
it post-acfh).

title: mantad admin API: expose scoped-access-key creation (parity with manta-ng)
description:
manta-ng supported bucket-scoped (and key-prefix-scoped) access keys: 
operators could mint an AK that the S3 gate treated as authoritative for a 
specific (bucket [, key-prefix]) tuple, bypassing the ACL/ownership 
fallback. mantad already carries the SAME data-plane model:

  AccessKey.scope: Vec<ScopeEntry>
  ScopeEntry { bucket, level: Read|ReadWrite|Full, key_prefix: Option<String> }

And the S3 gate already short-circuits on non-empty scope via
CallerContext::scope_authorized() at crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/
shared/caller.rs:77. The data-plane contract has integration coverage
(gate_tests::scope_authorized_true_for_nonempty_scope and 
scoped_caller_bypasses_acl_for_other_owner_in_same_workspace in the
same file).

GAP: the admin surface has no endpoint to mint a scoped key. Both
existing creation paths hard-code scope = Vec::new():

  crates/mantas3/cluster/src/admin.rs:856  (create_access_key)
  crates/mantas3/cluster/src/admin.rs:1856 (provision_workspace_presigner)

There's an in-source comment at line 855 acknowledging it:
  // Unscoped key: full workspace-admin privileges on the owning
  // user's workspace. Scoped-key creation is a separate admin
  // endpoint (follow-up bead — see plan).

FIX SHAPE:
  1. New admin endpoint:
     POST /admin/v1/users/{name}/scoped-access-keys
     body: { scope: [{ bucket, level, key_prefix? }, ...] }
     → mints a new AK with the requested scope on the user
  2. New tcadm subcommand:
     tcadm user scoped-key create <user> --bucket B --level read|rw|full        [--key-prefix PFX] [--bucket B2 --level ... ...]
     (mirrors manta-ng's scoped-key CLI)
  3. New admin-backend route to expose it in the webui

PRIORITY: P2 — parity feature with manta-ng. Backwards-compatible
addition; existing unscoped-key flows are unchanged.

EVIDENCE OF NEED: came up in 2026-06-03 review session — "do we
support bucket-scoped keys? manta-ng was supporting those".

title: manta-storage S3 surface: object-tagging input validation (key/value length, max-tags)
description:
Found by manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-object-tagging-test.sh against vnext mantad at 192.168.1.182:7443.

S3 spec limits for object tagging:
  - Tag key length: max 128 Unicode chars
  - Tag value length: max 256 Unicode chars
  - Max tags per object: 10

Mantad currently accepts payloads exceeding all three limits without
rejection (returns 200 instead of 400 InvalidTag / InvalidArgument).

Repro:
  S3_ENDPOINT=http://192.168.1.182:7443 \
    MANTA_USER=root AWS_REGION=us-east-1 \
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=<root-akid> AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=<root-secret> \
    bash manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-object-tagging-test.sh

Failing assertions (18/21 pass otherwise):
  FAIL Object tagging - Tag key too long should have been rejected but was accepted
  FAIL Object tagging - Tag value too long should have been rejected but was accepted
  FAIL Object tagging - Too many tags should have been rejected but was accepted

Fix shape: in PutObjectTagging / PutObject (when ?tagging= or
x-amz-tagging is present), validate each tag.key.len() <= 128,
tag.value.len() <= 256, and tag count <= 10. Return InvalidTag (400).

comments.author: neirac
comments.text:
Implemented in manta-storage 85c30fe on surface-s3.

FIX: tags_from_dto now takes a max_tags parameter and validates:
  - count <= max_tags (10 for objects, 50 for buckets per AWS spec)
  - key length 1..=128
  - value length 0..=256
  - non-empty key
  - no duplicate keys (preserved)

Errors: TooManyTags (HTTP 400) on count overflow; InvalidTag (HTTP
400) on every other violation. Both are real s3s error codes that
map to the right HTTP status.

UNIT COVERAGE: 9 new unit tests in mantas3-s3::s3_impl::shared::tags
covering happy path, all error branches, and the 128/256/10/50
boundaries.

LIVE VERIFY: stripped release mantad shipped to 192.168.1.182:7443
(sha 9d6898e9debef8724125922c948e7b73a94047dbd4d2eb177869afc47079e371)
and the manta-buckets-api object-tagging test went from 18/21 to
21/21:

  PASS: Object tagging - Tag key too long properly rejected
  PASS: Object tagging - Tag value too long properly rejected
  PASS: Object tagging - Too many tags properly rejected

title: manta-storage S3 surface: CompleteMultipartUpload accepts <5 MiB parts (missing EntityTooSmall)
description:
Found by manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-multipart-upload-test.sh against vnext mantad at 192.168.1.182:7443.

S3 spec: every part in a multipart upload except the last MUST be >= 5 MiB
(5,242,880 bytes). CompleteMultipartUpload should reject otherwise with
'EntityTooSmall'. Mantad currently accepts a 4 MiB intermediate part and
completes successfully.

Repro:
  S3_ENDPOINT=http://192.168.1.182:7443 \
    MANTA_USER=root AWS_REGION=us-east-1 \
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=<root-akid> AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=<root-secret> \
    bash manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-multipart-upload-test.sh

Failing assertion (10/11 pass otherwise):
  FAIL MPU error test - Complete should have failed with EntityTooSmall for 4MB parts

Fix shape: in CompleteMultipartUpload handler, after gathering the part
list, walk parts[0..len-1] and reject if any part_size < 5*1024*1024 with
EntityTooSmall (HTTP 400 + S3 XML error). Last part has no minimum.

comments.author: neirac
comments.text:
Implemented in manta-storage 36cbdbe on surface-s3.

FIX: In MantadS3::complete_multipart_upload_impl, after gathering
selected parts and before the concat call, walk parts[0..len-1] and
return EntityTooSmall if any intermediate part is < 5 MiB. Last part
and single-part uploads are exempt.

UNIT TESTS (new file: crates/mantas3/s3/tests/mpu_min_part_size.rs):
  - rejects 4 MiB intermediate part (EntityTooSmall) — the actual fix
  - allows single 1 KiB part (last-part exempt)
  - allows 5 MiB intermediate part (boundary inclusive)
All 3 pass locally.
