title: manta-storage S3 surface: anonymous GET on PAB-relaxed bucket returns 404
description:
Found by manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-acl-access-test.sh against vnext mantad at 192.168.1.182:7443.

After CHG-193 ships the cross-tenant public-bucket gate, the data-plane still
needs to honor PAB relaxation for anonymous GET. The test creates a bucket
named 'public', uploads an object with public-read ACL via the authenticated
caller, then issues an anonymous GET — mantad returns HTTP 404 instead of
serving the object.

Repro:
  S3_ENDPOINT=http://192.168.1.182:7443 \
    MANTA_USER=root AWS_REGION=us-east-1 \
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=<root-akid> AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=<root-secret> \
    bash manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-acl-access-test.sh

Failing assertions:
  FAIL Anonymous access - Failed to access object in 'public' bucket (HTTP 404)
  FAIL Public ACL anonymous access test - Failed to upload object with public-read ACL

Likely fix shape: GET/HEAD path checks PublicAccessBlock on the bucket; when
relaxed AND the bucket is the canonical name owner per CHG-193 idx, allow
anonymous reads. ACL-based public-read (canned ACL) is the secondary lever.
close_reason: wontfix: mantad anonymous-read path works correctly when bucket is properly configured (PAB relaxed + public-read bucket policy → 200). Live-verified. Both test failures are test-side bugs: legacy Manta-URL format + missing PAB-relax step.

comments.author: neirac
comments.text:
Investigated 2026-06-03 against vnext mantad on 192.168.1.182:7443.

ROOT CAUSE: Two test failures, both test-side bugs.

(1) test_anonymous_access_public_bucket — uses a Manta-legacy URL:

    ${S3_ENDPOINT}/${MANTA_USER}/buckets/${public_bucket}/objects/${test_object}
    → http://192.168.1.182:7443/root/buckets/public/objects/anon-test.txt

That path was served by the old Node.js manta-buckets-api, which
multiplexed Manta REST API + S3 on the same listener. Mantad's s3s
listener is pure S3 (path-style and virtual-host-style only), so this
URL hits no route and returns NoSuchBucket. The test needs to use the
standard S3 URL: \${S3_ENDPOINT}/\${bucket}/\${key}.

(2) test_anonymous_access_public_acl — tries:

    aws s3api put-object --bucket private-bucket --key x --body x --acl public-read

This fails with HTTP 403 AccessDenied
'block_public_acls=true: public ACLs are blocked'. That's the
post-2023 AWS S3 default for new buckets: BlockPublicAcls=true. The
test was written before AWS enabled that default. Real AWS S3 returns
the same error today. To upload a public-read-ACL object, the bucket's
PAB must first be relaxed (PutPublicAccessBlock with
BlockPublicAcls=false).

VERIFICATION: The underlying anonymous-read path works correctly when
the bucket is properly configured. Live-probed sequence:

  1. aws s3api delete-public-access-block --bucket public
  2. aws s3api put-public-access-block --bucket public
       --public-access-block-configuration
       '{\"BlockPublicAcls\":false,\"IgnorePublicAcls\":false,
         \"BlockPublicPolicy\":false,\"RestrictPublicBuckets\":false}'
  3. aws s3api put-bucket-policy --bucket public --policy
       '{\"Version\":\"2012-10-17\",\"Statement\":[
         {\"Effect\":\"Allow\",\"Principal\":\"*\",
          \"Action\":\"s3:GetObject\",
          \"Resource\":\"arn:aws:s3:::public/*\"}]}'
  4. curl http://192.168.1.182:7443/public/anon-test.txt
     → HTTP/1.1 200 OK + body 'hello world'

PAB-relaxation + public-read bucket-policy + anonymous GET works
end-to-end on the current build. CHG-193 + Phase D wired this
correctly.

RESOLUTION: wontfix. The test should be modernized to:
  - Use the standard S3 URL for anonymous GET
  - Set BlockPublicAcls=false on the bucket before
    put-object --acl public-read

title: tcadm auth integration test missing User.capabilities field
description: cli/tcadm/tests/auth.rs:42 constructs a tritond_store::User struct literal without the capabilities field. The field was added at libs/tritond-store/src/types.rs:116 (pub capabilities: BTreeSet<Capability>) but the test wasn't updated. `cargo test --no-run -p tcadm` fails with E0063. Fix: add `capabilities: BTreeSet::new(),` to the literal (root migration semantics expand empty -> Capability::all() at auth time, so empty initial set is the intended bootstrap state per types.rs:113-115).
close_reason: Resolved in commit 1e9eb25b on surface-s3 via workspace OpenSpec change CHG-199 (workspace bd beads-2zov).

title: drop_silo_tenant_storage: presigner-system user not cascaded on workspace delete
description: Reproducer: tcadm tenant drop-storage <silo> <tenant> returns 409 Conflict with mantad error 'WorkspaceNotEmpty: workspace t-<uuid> still has IAM user presigner-t-<uuid> (and possibly others); delete IAM users before archiving'. The Phase 2 design (plan §Phase 2 §3 cache invalidation) assumed mantad would cascade the presigner-system user when delete_workspace is called; in practice mantad refuses non-empty workspaces and the presigner-system user counts as 'not empty'. Result: drop-storage never reaches presigner_cache.evict on tritond, leaving the cache potentially stale across rebinds and orphaning tenants whose only IAM user is the system presigner. Fix options: (a) mantad-side — recognize and auto-cascade *-system users on delete_workspace; (b) tritond-side — have drop_silo_tenant_storage explicitly call delete_user for presigner-<workspace> before delete_workspace. (b) is the smaller blast radius. Discovered while running tools/s3-presigner-cache-fallback-verify.py 2026-05-31.
close_reason: fixed in archive_tenant_workspace: explicit delete of presigner-{workspace} IAM user before delete_workspace. Live-verified with tools/s3-presigner-cache-fallback-verify.py --skip-n4w7-workaround on 192.168.1.182; drop-storage cascades the system user end-to-end, cache evict path engages, AKIDs delta proven (MKIAZ7MJ vs MKIAYDLR). Evidence: evidence/n4w7-presigner-cascade-fix-20260531.md.

title: Tritond-mediated presign integration live verify
description: Drive tritond's /v1/storage/clusters/{cluster_id}/presign-{put,get} endpoints with an authenticated tenant principal, against a live cluster. Prove that the Phase 2 cache + scope threading + drop-storage eviction work end-to-end through tritond (Phase 2 evidence so far is mantad-side direct; the tritond integration is unit-tested only).

title: Phase 2: per-workspace presigner credentials
description: Replace tritond's cluster-root presigner with a per-workspace IAM-style access key minted on mantad. Closes the data-plane workspace gate's last bypass (presigned URLs currently authenticate as root). Mantad-side authoritative secret + tritond in-process cache (5-min TTL). See ~/.claude/plans/now-that-we-know-harmonic-twilight.md rev 2 §Phase 2.

title: Stage D: tidy ApiContext (telemetry + background sub-structs)
Centralize rustls crypto provider install
description: reqwest is built with rustls-no-provider at the workspace level, so every binary that builds a reqwest::Client (directly or via a Progenitor client like mahi-client) has to call rustls::crypto::ring::default_provider().install_default() in main() before anything else. We have this duplicated in triton-gateway, bugview-service, and now triton-api-server; library tests have a parallel problem. A cleaner fix would be either (a) a tiny triton-rustls-provider crate whose ctor installs the provider, included as a workspace dep on anything that pulls reqwest, or (b) fold the install into an existing triton-tls helper. File the pattern audit once a third or fourth duplicate shows up.

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: Hit again on triton-api-server during the mahi refactor: the 'no provider set' crypto panic AND the 'No CA certificates were loaded from the system' panic from reqwest::Client::builder().build(). Both fixed inline for now (d53f508 installs the ring provider; 35aad6a wires triton-tls::build_http_client through MahiService). The CA-store case is what tritonadm already routes through triton-tls, so the durable fix likely wants to fold both into a single 'build the canonical reqwest client for this workspace' helper.

title: mahi: verify GET /users?fallback on-wire format and omission vs null user
description:
During Phase 5 validation of the mahi conversion we confirmed the API type (`AuthInfo { user: Option<User>, roles: HashMap<_,_>, ... }`) can accept either a null user field or a missing user key; serde skip_serializing_if on `user` is not currently applied, so the server would emit `"user": null` on fallback. Phase 1 listed two open questions that still need wire-level verification against the running Node.js service:

1. Does GET /users?fallback=true with a missing sub-user return `{"user": null, ...}` or omit the key entirely?
2. Do real node-mahi clients send fallback as the string "true" or boolean true? (Our Query struct accepts both because serde's Option<bool> parses both via JSON, but query parsers coerce strings — worth confirming against a real node-mahi request.)

Blocks nothing today (CloudAPI and node-triton don't use fallback), but should be resolved before any Rust mahi-server implementation attempts to preserve the upstream wire behavior exactly.

title: mahi: verify wire format of SigV4VerifyResult.signingKey
description: During Phase 5 validation of the mahi conversion, the wire format of `SigV4VerifyResult.signingKey` was not confirmed against live callers. The field is currently typed as `Option<serde_json::Value>` in `apis/mahi-api/src/types/sigv4.rs` because Node.js emits a Buffer JSON envelope (`{"type":"Buffer","data":[...]}`). Before any consumer relies on this field, check how (if at all) real clients consume it and either tighten the type (bytes/hex string) or drop the field from the response schema. Phase 1 flagged this explicitly as 'Verify with test/integration/sigv4-sts-flow.test.js during Phase 5.'

title: triton-api-server: 1 MiB body limit too small for CloudAPI compat
description:
services/triton-api-server/src/main.rs:102 sets default_request_body_max_bytes: 1024 * 1024 (1 MiB) on ConfigDropshot. CloudAPI accepts significantly larger request bodies — user-script payloads, machine metadata blobs, image manifests, and volume configs can all exceed this.

Once tritonapi starts serving CloudAPI-compat endpoints natively (Phase 2+), clients submitting legitimate large payloads will get 413 Payload Too Large where the old CloudAPI accepted the request.

Fix: raise the limit to match CloudAPI's (needs investigation — check Node.js CloudAPI config, likely 5-10 MiB). Make it configurable via the SAPI config file so it can be tuned without rebuilds.

Also relevant for the gateway: there is no body size limit configured there at all. Decide whether the gateway should enforce a max (defense in depth) or rely on the backend to reject.

Acceptance: the limit is at least as permissive as CloudAPI's, and is sourced from config rather than hardcoded.

title: triton-gateway: no graceful shutdown on SIGTERM
description:
services/triton-gateway/src/main.rs calls axum::serve(listener, app).await? with no signal handling. SMF's stop method is ':kill' which sends SIGTERM; the current implementation drops all in-flight requests when it receives one.

Fix: use axum::serve(listener, app).with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal()) where shutdown_signal() awaits a tokio::signal::ctrl_c() OR a unix SIGTERM from tokio::signal::unix. Give in-flight requests a bounded drain period (e.g., 30 seconds to match SMF's timeout_seconds), then force-exit.

Same fix should be applied to triton-api-server (Dropshot has its own shutdown story — check the Dropshot docs for the equivalent).

Acceptance: issuing 'svcadm disable triton-gateway' during a long-running request lets the request finish (up to the timeout) before the process exits.

title: triton-gateway: no request-ID generation or propagation
description:
The gateway has no request-ID handling. For a reverse proxy fronting 183 CloudAPI endpoints plus new tritonapi endpoints, request-ID correlation is table stakes for debugging and audit trails.

Fix: add middleware that:
1. Checks inbound request for X-Request-Id (or x-request-id); if absent, generate a UUIDv4
2. Attaches the request ID to a tracing span so all log lines for this request include it
3. Forwards the X-Request-Id header to the backend
4. Echoes X-Request-Id in the response

Backends (tritonapi, CloudAPI) should preserve the header they receive. Retrofitting this later is expensive — adding it now is cheap.

Consider also adopting W3C traceparent/tracestate for OpenTelemetry compatibility if the broader Triton stack wants that.

Acceptance: a request with X-Request-Id: foo logs 'foo' on every gateway log line for that request, and the same ID appears in the backend's logs.

title: Config loading silently falls back to defaults on parse/IO error
description:
Both services have a load_config() that logs an error and returns defaults when TRITON__CONFIG_FILE is set but unreadable or unparseable:
- services/triton-gateway/src/main.rs:109-129
- services/triton-api-server/src/main.rs:48-68

For an SMF service rendered by config-agent, silent fallback is the wrong posture. A malformed template or missing file should fail fast so SMF marks the service in maintenance and the operator notices. The current behavior produces a 'healthy' service listening on the wrong port or proxying to the wrong backend.

Fix: make load_config() return Result<Config, Error>. In main(), propagate the error. Keep the default-when-env-unset behavior (that's a legitimate dev case), but fail hard when the env var is set and the file cannot be read or parsed.

Acceptance: a syntactically invalid /opt/triton/triton-api/etc/config.json causes the service to exit non-zero; SMF shows maintenance state with the parse error in the log.

title: SMF: triton-gateway runs as root (should drop to nobody)
description:
images/triton-api/smf/manifests/triton-gateway.xml has no <method_credential> block, so the gateway process runs as root. It binds 0.0.0.0:80 which only needs net_privaddr, not root.

A compromise of the gateway currently gives root in the zone. Mariana-trench's triton-admin manifest drops to user=nobody group=nobody privileges=basic,net_privaddr which is the pattern to follow.

Fix: add to the start exec_method's <method_context>:
<method_credential user="nobody" group="nobody" privileges="basic,net_privaddr"/>

The triton-api-server manifest can stay as-is (it only binds loopback) but should probably also be hardened for consistency — file separate issue if desired.

Acceptance: 'ps -o user,comm' in the zone shows triton-gateway running as 'nobody'.

title: SMF: missing config-agent dependency on triton-api and triton-gateway
description:
Neither images/triton-api/smf/manifests/triton-api.xml nor triton-gateway.xml declares a dependency on svc:/smartdc/application/config-agent:default. The services can start before config-agent has rendered their SAPI templates, causing them to load defaults and only pick up the real config when SAPI's post_cmd issues a restart.

Combined with the silent config-fallback behavior (separate issue), this creates a first-boot window where services appear healthy but are misconfigured.

Fix: add to both SMF manifests:
<dependency name="config-agent" grouping="require_all" restart_on="none" type="service">
    <service_fmri value="svc:/smartdc/application/config-agent:default"/>
</dependency>

Reference: mariana-trench/images/triton-admin/smf/manifests/triton-admin.xml shows this pattern.

Acceptance: on a fresh zone boot, services start only after config-agent is online and the config file exists.

title: SMF: gateway→triton-api dependency should be restart_on=none
description:
images/triton-api/smf/manifests/triton-gateway.xml declares a dependency on triton-api with restart_on="restart". This means any config change or restart of triton-api cascades to a gateway restart.

Problems:
- Changing triton-api config via SAPI drops in-flight gateway proxy requests to CloudAPI for no reason
- If triton-api crashloops, the gateway restarts with it, breaking the invariant that 'the gateway keeps proxying to CloudAPI even when tritonapi is down'

Fix: change restart_on to 'none'. require_all ensures startup ordering, which is enough — the gateway only reads triton-api's OpenAPI spec at startup (eventually, per the design doc route-list-from-spec plan).

Acceptance: 'svcadm restart triton-api' does not affect the gateway process.

title: triton-gateway: URL path joining can produce double-slash
description:
services/triton-gateway/src/main.rs line ~181: format!("{}{}", base_url, path) concatenates the configured base_url with the request path_and_query. If base_url ends in '/' and path starts with '/', the result contains '//' which some backends reject or route differently.

Fix: parse base_url into a hyper::Uri once at startup, extract the authority, and build the proxied URI with Uri::builder().scheme(...).authority(...).path_and_query(path). This also enables fixing the Host header (separate issue) since the authority is available.

Acceptance: cloudapi_url configured as 'http://cloudapi.dc.example.com/' (trailing slash) produces correct single-slash URIs for incoming requests.

title: triton-gateway: WebSocket proxying not implemented
description:
CloudAPI exposes three WebSocket endpoints that the gateway must proxy for clients using JWT or HTTP Signature auth:
- /{account}/changefeed
- /{account}/migrations/{machine}/watch
- /{account}/machines/{machine}/vnc

The current forward_request in services/triton-gateway/src/main.rs strips Upgrade and Connection as hop-by-hop headers (which is correct for regular HTTP-over-HTTP), so any WebSocket upgrade attempt silently falls back to HTTP. The client never gets a 101 response.

Fix: detect WebSocket upgrades (Upgrade: websocket header) and handle them separately. Axum provides on_upgrade() for bidirectional tunneling. Options:
1. Use axum's WebSocketUpgrade extractor and tokio-tungstenite on the backend side to proxy frames.
2. Use hyper::upgrade::on() on both sides to tunnel raw bytes without parsing WebSocket frames (simpler, avoids frame translation overhead).

Option 2 is closer to a pure proxy. Document decision in code.

Acceptance: wscat to a /{account}/changefeed endpoint via the gateway receives a valid WebSocket handshake and can exchange frames with CloudAPI.

title: triton-gateway: missing X-Forwarded-For / Forwarded headers
description:
services/triton-gateway/src/main.rs forward_request() does not add any of the standard proxy forwarding headers before passing the request to the backend. CloudAPI audit logs will record the gateway's admin IP as the source of every request.

Fix: add these headers to the proxied request (appending to existing if present):
- X-Forwarded-For: <client-IP>
- X-Forwarded-Proto: http|https
- X-Forwarded-Host: <original Host>
- X-Real-IP: <client-IP>
- Optionally RFC 7239 Forwarded: for=<client>;proto=<proto>;host=<host>

Getting the peer address requires axum's ConnectInfo<SocketAddr> extractor, which needs Router::into_make_service_with_connect_info(). See axum docs.

Acceptance: a request from 10.0.0.5 to the gateway arrives at CloudAPI with X-Forwarded-For: 10.0.0.5 (or appended if a previous proxy set it).

title: triton-gateway: Host header not rewritten when proxying
description:
In services/triton-gateway/src/main.rs forward_request() (around line 168-197), headers from the inbound request are copied verbatim to the upstream request. The Host header is preserved, pointing at the gateway's public hostname rather than the backend's authority.

Problems:
- CloudAPI performs virtual-host routing / logs Host; proxied requests may be misrouted or logged incorrectly
- For tritonapi at 127.0.0.1:8080, the backend sees a Host header for the public domain
- HTTP Signature verification: if the client signed 'host' in the signature headers list, the backend will re-hash the same Host string the client signed, so passthrough is actually correct for signatures. But CloudAPI-specific routing may still be wrong.

Fix: parse base_url into a URI once at startup, set the proxied request's Host header to base_url's authority. Consider making this configurable so HTTP Signature pass-through still works where Host is signed.

Acceptance: test that a request with Host: gateway.example.com proxied to CloudAPI arrives with Host: cloudapi.<dc>.<domain>, and that a signed request still verifies if Host was covered.

title: PingResponse empty and inconsistent with other API crates
description: PingResponse serializes to {}. VMAPI has status/healthy/backend_status fields. Also missing Clone derive. Types inline in lib.rs instead of types/ module - diverges from cloudapi-api, vmapi-api, bugview-api patterns.

title: triton-api not registered in openapi-manager
description: Every other API crate is registered in openapi-manager/src/main.rs. triton-api is missing, breaking make openapi-generate/openapi-check pattern. Register or add tracked TODO.

title: Design doc uses REPO_ROOT, impl uses ENGBLD_REPO_ROOT
description: All code blocks in docs/design/zone-image-builds.md reference REPO_ROOT but actual implementation uses ENGBLD_REPO_ROOT. Update doc to match implementation.

title: Template READMEs left untracked
description: apis/triton-api/README.md and services/triton-api-server/README.md still contain template boilerplate. Either commit with real content, remove, or gitignore.
close_reason: wontfix: files don't exist in the repo, were untracked template artifacts

title: P2: Missing multi-item list fixtures and ErrorResponse fixture
description:
Only Machine and Image have multi-item list fixtures (instance_list_multi.json, image_list_multi.json). All other resources (Network, Volume, Package, Disk, NIC, Snapshot, Key, FirewallRule) only have single-object fixtures. This means tests cannot verify list sorting, filtering, or multi-item rendering.

Additionally missing:
- Empty list response fixture ([]) for testing empty list handling
- ErrorResponse fixture for error handling tests
- Null optional field fixtures (e.g., Machine with memory: null for LX zones)
- Fixtures for Account, User, AccessKey, FabricVlan, NetworkIp, Migration, AuditEntry, Datacenter

Directory: cli/triton-cli/tests/fixtures/

title: P2: Untested parser functions: parse_nic_opts, parse_nic_spec, apply_positional_filters
description:
Several non-trivial parser functions in triton-cli have zero unit test coverage:

1. parse_nic_opts (cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/nic.rs) - parses NIC configuration options. Only netmask_to_cidr is tested.
2. parse_nic_spec (cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/create.rs) - parses NIC specification strings. Async but parsing logic could be factored out.
3. apply_positional_filters (cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs) - parses user-facing key=value filter syntax for volume list.

These functions handle user input and could have edge case bugs (missing =, empty keys, special characters) that go undetected.

title: P2: No negative API tests for invalid UUIDs across get commands
description:
No integration test passes an invalid or nonexistent UUID to any 'get' command (image get, network get, package get, key get, fwrule get, vlan get, instance get). These are the most likely places for error handling regressions. At minimum, test that each 'get' command with a nonexistent UUID produces a meaningful error message (not a panic or empty output).

Files: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_images.rs, cli_networks.rs, cli_packages.rs, cli_keys.rs, cli_fwrules.rs, cli_vlans.rs

title: P2: Missing snake_case exception fixtures for dns_names, free_space, delegate_dataset
description:
CLAUDE.md documents dns_names, free_space, and delegate_dataset as important Machine struct snake_case exceptions (fields that use explicit #[serde(rename)] to keep snake_case despite the struct's rename_all = camelCase). No machine fixture exercises any of these fields. If the serde rename annotations were removed, no test would catch the regression.

Fix: add a machine fixture (or extend instance_list_multi.json) that includes these fields with realistic values. dns_names should be an array of DNS names, free_space should be a number (for bhyve flexible disk VMs), delegate_dataset should be a boolean.
Files: cli/triton-cli/tests/fixtures/machine/instance_list.json or instance_list_multi.json

title: P3: Short ID resolution fetches all machines (performance at scale)
description:
For short ID resolution, Rust calls list_machines() which fetches all machines in one request. For accounts with many machines, this could be very slow or hit pagination limits. node-triton uses a streaming createListMachinesStream() for short ID matching.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/get.rs:73-79
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/tritonapi.js:1554

Fix: Consider pagination or streaming approach for large accounts.

title: P3: No table header/column validation tests for list commands
description:
Output format tests focus on JSON structure. No tests verify table column headers, column ordering, or --long column sets match node-triton's expected output.

Fix: Add table output tests that verify column headers for key list commands (instance list, image list, network list, etc.).

title: P3: cli_disks.rs has only 2 tests (help only), no behavioral tests
description:
Disk commands (list, get, resize, delete) have zero offline behavioral tests. Only 2 help-output tests exist.

File: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_disks.rs

Fix: Add fixture data and offline tests for disk operations.

title: P3: Short ID resolution doesn't normalize docker-style IDs
description:
node-triton's normShortId reformats docker-style container IDs (inserting dashes to form UUID prefix format). Rust does simple starts_with on the raw string. Docker users accustomed to 'triton instance get abc123def456' format may get not found.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/get.rs:68-72
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/common.js:434-458

Fix: Low priority — document the difference or add docker-style normalization.

title: P3: triton info instance state ordering is non-deterministic
description:
Uses HashMap for state counts, which has random iteration order. The instance state breakdown varies between runs. node-triton uses insertion order.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/info.rs:46-49

Fix: Use BTreeMap for sorted, deterministic output.

title: P3: Instance create output missing image/package details
description:
node-triton prints 'Creating instance NAME (UUID, IMAGE@VERSION, PACKAGE)' including resolved image name@version and package. Rust prints 'Creating instance NAME (SHORT_UUID)' — omits resolved image and package names.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/create.rs:304
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/do_instance/do_create.js:372-374

Fix: Include resolved image name@version and package name in create output.

title: P2: cli_subcommands.rs tests only check non-empty output (218 weak assertions)
description:
218 tests (~34% of all tests) only assert .stdout(predicate::str::is_empty().not()) — verifies something was printed but not correctness. These pass even if help output is garbage.

File: cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_subcommands.rs

Fix: Add predicate::str::contains("Usage:") to help-output tests.

title: P2: rbac apply dev-mode generates ed25519 keys vs node-triton RSA-4096
description:
node-triton generates RSA-4096 keys (ssh-keygen -t rsa -m PEM -b 4096). Rust generates ed25519 keys. Modern and better, but older SmartOS/CloudAPI may not support ed25519.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:1142-1143
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/rbac.js:810-811

Fix: Low priority — ed25519 is reasonable modernization. Document the change. Consider RSA fallback for older infra.
close_reason: Added --dev-key-type flag (ed25519 default, rsa fallback) to rbac apply

title: P2: Missing fixture files for disk, NIC, snapshot, key, fwrule resource types
description:
No JSON fixtures exist for: disks, NICs, snapshots, SSH keys, firewall rules. These resource types can only be tested with API access (integration tests). Other resource types (machine, image, network, volume, package) have fixtures.

Directory: cli/triton-cli/tests/fixtures/

Fix: Add JSON fixture files for each missing resource type, then add offline tests using them.

title: P2: UUID resolve comment incorrectly claims matching node-triton
description:
Comment on line 61 says 'matching node-triton's behavior' but node-triton actually calls getMachine(uuid) to verify existence and handles 404/410. Rust returns the parsed UUID without any API call. The comment is misleading.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/get.rs:60-63
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/tritonapi.js:1503-1524

Fix: At minimum fix the misleading comment. Optionally verify UUID exists server-side.

title: P2: triton env drops null docker env values silently
description:
node-triton emits 'unset KEY' for null docker env values. Rust's filter_map skips null values entirely — no unset command generated. If docker setup.json has DOCKER_TLS_VERIFY: null, the environment variable won't be cleaned up.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs:37
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/do_env.js:105-111

Fix: Handle null values by emitting unset commands.
Test: Test with fixture containing null docker env values.

title: P2: Debug format {:?} in user-facing --show-headers output
description:
cloudapi.rs uses Debug format for HTTP version in --show-headers output: println!("{:?} {}", response.version(), response.status()). Shows HTTP/1.1 as HTTP11 or similar Debug representation.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/cloudapi.rs:159

Fix: Use Display trait or manual formatting for HTTP version.
close_reason: wontfix: http::Version Debug output is already user-friendly (HTTP/1.1 etc.); added comment documenting the allowed exception

title: P2: rbac role-tags set/remove/clear missing confirmation prompts
description:
node-triton prompts 'Set role tags on <type> "<id>"? [y/n]' (skippable with -y). Rust executes immediately without confirmation. Destructive role-tag operations (especially clear which removes all tags) have no safety net.

Files: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/role_tags.rs:130 (set), :272 (remove), :294 (clear)
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/do_rbac/do_role_tags.js:257-271

Fix: Add -y/--yes flag; prompt for confirmation when not in non-interactive mode.

title: P1: rbac info --all silently swallows key fetch errors
description:
In rbac info --all, individual key fetch failures are silently swallowed: `if let Ok(keys) = keys_result`. A failed key fetch for one user silently results in that user appearing to have 0 keys with no warning or error output. This could mislead operators into thinking a user has no keys when actually the API call failed.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:237-239

Fix: Log a warning when key fetch fails, or include an error indicator in the output.
Test: Mock server returning 500 for one user's keys.

title: P1: Image list flags missing X and + (S flag conflates two meanings)
description:
node-triton computes 5 flag types: I (has origin), P (public), X (non-active state), + (shared by you - owner matches account), S (shared with you - account in ACL). Rust computes only 3: I (origin), S (any ACL present), P (public). Missing X and + flags; S conflates two distinct meanings.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs:467-480
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/do_image/do_list.js:129-147

Fix: Add X flag for non-active state. Differentiate + vs S by comparing image owner to current account UUID.
Test: Add fixture-based test for flag computation with various ACL/owner combinations.

title: P1: triton env --docker silently empty when docker not set up
description:
node-triton raises ConfigError when docker is explicitly requested (-d) but setup.json is missing, suggesting user run triton profile docker-setup. Rust silently outputs '# docker' with no variables and no error.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs:197-202
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/do_env.js:121-126

Fix: When --docker is explicitly passed and setup.json is missing, return an error with setup instructions.
Test: Test triton env --docker with no setup.json.

title: P1: rbac apply silently ignores SSH key configuration
description:
node-triton's rbac apply handles per-user SSH key CRUD as part of the apply workflow (create-key, update-key, delete-key operations). The config file can specify keys per user (as a file path or inline). Rust's RbacConfigUser struct has no keys field — key specifications in rbac.json are silently dropped by serde.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs:58-67 (RbacConfigUser struct)
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/rbac.js:259-335 (loadUserKeys), :443-533 (key CRUD in plan)

Fix: Add keys field to RbacConfigUser, implement key comparison and CRUD in the apply plan.
Test: Test apply with user key configuration.

title: P1: triton env missing SDC_TESTING export for insecure profiles
description:
node-triton conditionally exports SDC_TESTING based on profile.insecure. Rust unconditionally unsets SDC_TESTING in all three shell formats (bash, fish, powershell). Profiles with insecure: true (self-signed certs) will break legacy sdc-* tools when using eval $(triton env).

Files: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs:222 (bash), :293 (fish), :363 (powershell)
Comparison: target/node-triton/lib/do_env.js:149-153

Fix: Read profile insecure setting; conditionally emit export SDC_TESTING=1 vs unset SDC_TESTING.
Test: Test env output with insecure profile fixture.

title: P1: Reboot --wait can return false positive (race condition)
description:
Reboot --wait calls wait_for_state(Running), which polls getMachine until state is running. If the instance was already running before the reboot API call is processed, wait_for_state returns immediately even though the reboot hasn't happened yet. node-triton uses waitForMachineAudit to check the audit trail for a successful reboot action, which is definitive.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/wait.rs:73-99
Comparison: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/lifecycle.rs:104-126 vs target/node-triton/lib/do_instance_reboot.js

Fix: For reboot specifically, record a timestamp before the action and verify via audit trail or by checking that the machine transitioned through a non-running state.
close_reason: Fixed: reboot --wait now uses audit-trail polling instead of ambiguous state==running check

title: P0: Multi-target lifecycle commands exit on first error, skipping remaining instances
description:
lifecycle.rs start/stop/reboot iterate targets with `for instance in &args.instances` and use `?` to propagate errors immediately. If `instance start inst1 inst2 inst3` fails on inst1, inst2 and inst3 are never attempted. node-triton attempts ALL targets and collects errors.

File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/lifecycle.rs:56-77 (start), :80-101 (stop), :104-126 (reboot)

Fix: Collect errors in a Vec, attempt all targets, report all failures at the end, exit non-zero if any failed.
Test: Mock server returning 404 for first instance, 202 for others — verify all are attempted.
close_reason: Fixed: lifecycle and delete commands now collect errors across all instances instead of exiting on first failure

title: Missing serde rename override on dangerous_allow_shrink in ResizeDiskRequest
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine_resources.rs, line 164
ResizeDiskRequest struct (line 158, rename_all = "camelCase")

Field dangerous_allow_shrink auto-renames to dangerousAllowShrink, but wire format is dangerous_allow_shrink.

Evidence: VMAPI conversion plan (conversion-plans/vmapi/plan.md, line 251) shows dangerous_allow_shrink.

Note: This is a latent issue - the struct is not yet wired to an API endpoint, but will cause
problems when the endpoint is connected.

Fix: Add #[serde(rename = "dangerous_allow_shrink")] to the field.

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=cloudapi-api

title: Hardcoded enum string "tritonnfs" in volume.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs, line 86
The clap default_value uses hardcoded string "tritonnfs" matching VolumeType::Tritonnfs wire name.

Current code:
    #[arg(long, short = 't', default_value = "tritonnfs")]
    pub r#type: VolumeType,

Suggested fix - use default_value_t for type safety:
    #[arg(long, short = 't', default_value_t = VolumeType::Tritonnfs)]
    pub r#type: VolumeType,

This pattern is already used elsewhere (e.g., default_value_t = DEFAULT_CERT_LIFETIME_DAYS in profile.rs:106).

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli

title: Add automatic pagination (LOMStream equivalent) to cloudapi-client
description: The Rust CLI makes single requests for list endpoints and returns whatever the server gives back. node-triton uses LOMStream which automatically pages through results in 1000-item chunks (limit+offset) until exhausted. Without client-side pagination, users with >1000 machines/images/etc get silently truncated results. The Progenitor-generated builders already accept .limit() and .offset(), so the plumbing exists — we need a pagination helper in cloudapi-client that wraps list endpoints and loops until the response has fewer items than the limit.

title: payload-vlan-update: Node emits empty payload (bug), Rust emits correct PUT
description: In the vlan update payload test, Node emits an empty payload [] and exits 1 because tritonapi.updateFabricVlan is not a function (a bug in node-triton). Rust correctly emits a PUT request to update the VLAN. This is a known Node bug — the Rust implementation is correct.
close_reason: Disabled comparison test: node-triton has a bug (tritonapi.updateFabricVlan is not a function), so there is nothing valid to compare against. Rust implementation is correct.

title: payload-image-copy: Node resolves image+datacenters, Rust sends POST directly
description: In the image copy payload test, Node does GET /images/<uuid> then GET /datacenters (to resolve the target DC) before failing (exit 1). Rust sends POST /images directly (exit 0). Node creates a separate client targeting the destination DC, which the emit-payload fake server can't handle. The Rust implementation sends the copy request directly to the current DC's API. Both approaches may be valid but produce different payloads.

title: VLAN list silently ignores unknown filter field names
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/vlan.rs:39, the _ => {} match arm silently ignores unrecognized filter field names. A user typing 'triton vlan list nmae=myvlan' (typo) gets all VLANs returned unfiltered with no indication the filter was ignored. Should bail with an error listing valid field names, matching the pattern used in field=value update parsers (fwrule.rs, image.rs) which correctly use anyhow::bail\!("unknown field: {key}").

title: No serde tests for MemberRef, PolicyRef, MemberType RBAC types
description:
The new structured RBAC types in apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/user.rs have no serde serialization/deserialization tests:

- MemberRef with #[serde(rename = "type")] on member_type field
- PolicyRef with optional name and id fields
- MemberType with #[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]

These replace the previous Vec<String> fields in CreateRoleRequest/UpdateRoleRequest, making this a wire-format breaking change for CloudAPI v9.0.0+. If the serde rename on member_type broke (serializing as "member_type" instead of "type"), role creation would silently fail server-side. Add roundtrip tests in apis/cloudapi-api/tests/serde_actions.rs or a new serde_user.rs.

title: Inconsistent image action dispatch: query param vs body
description:
In clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs, image action methods use inconsistent approaches for sending the action parameter:

- export_image (line 1296): uses ActionBody to put action in body
- update_image_metadata (line 1277): uses .action() query parameter
- clone_image (line 1327): uses .action() query parameter
- import_image_from_datacenter (line 1352): uses .action() query parameter

All machine/volume/disk actions consistently use ActionBody. The image methods diverge without comments explaining why. Either standardize on one approach or add per-method comments documenting the wire-format rationale (e.g. clone_image has a comment about matching node-triton, but export_image has none explaining its different approach).

title: Missing #[serde(other)] on 5 CloudAPI action/type enums
description:
Five enums in cloudapi-api lack the #[serde(other)] Unknown catch-all variant required by CLAUDE.md Rule 5 for enums deserializing untrusted input. These are action-dispatch enums deserialized from query params and request bodies:

- MachineAction (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs:440)
- ImageAction (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs:216)
- DiskAction (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine_resources.rs:144)
- VolumeAction (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs:120)
- MemberType (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/user.rs:162)

VmAction in vmapi-api already has the correct pattern. If new actions are added server-side, deserialization will fail instead of gracefully handling the unknown variant.

title: node-triton patch: emit-payload gaps for disk/vlan delete
description:
Tests affected: payload-disk-delete, payload-vlan-delete
Node exits 1 with empty output for these commands in emit-payload mode - the node-triton patch doesn't handle delete code paths for disks and VLANs.
Rust correctly emits DELETE payloads.
Fix: Update the node-triton emit-payload patch to handle disk delete and vlan delete subcommands.

title: node-triton patch: trailing empty objects in tag/metadata set
description:
Tests affected: payload-tag-set, payload-tag-replace, payload-meta-set
The node-triton emit-payload patch emits trailing {} empty objects in the request array for these commands. This is a patch artifact, not a Rust bug.
Fix: Update the node-triton emit-payload patch to suppress empty trailing objects for tag and metadata code paths.
close_reason: Fixed in 4edc042: jq filter select(.method) drops trailing {} objects

title: CLI compat: verification GET consistency (resize, image-delete)
description:
Two tests show inconsistent verification GET behavior:
1. payload-resize: Node verifies package exists with GET /packages before sending resize action. Rust skips the verification GET.
2. payload-image-delete: Rust has an extra verification GET before DELETE that Node doesn't do.
Fix: Match Node's behavior for both - add verification GET for resize, remove extra GET for image-delete.

title: CLI compat: NIC add/remove wire format differences
description:
Two NIC-related payload comparison failures:
1. payload-nic-add: Rust omits the default 'primary': false field that Node includes. Also exit code differs (node=1, rust=0).
2. payload-nic-remove: Node strips colons from MAC address in URL path (aabbccddeeff), Rust preserves them (aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff). Node's behavior matches CloudAPI expectation.
Fix: Add primary:false default to NIC add payload, strip colons from MAC in NIC remove URL path.

title: CLI compat: image copy semantics differ from node-triton
description:
payload-image-copy test shows fundamental semantic difference:
- Node does GET image + GET datacenters, then exits 1 (image copy is not fully implemented in emit-payload mode)
- Rust sends POST with import-from-datacenter action directly, exits 0
- Need to decide: match Node's incomplete behavior, or keep Rust's correct-but-different approach
- May require updating the node-triton emit-payload patch to handle image copy properly

title: CLI compat: image action wire format (clone, share, unshare)
description: node-triton sends image actions via query param (?action=clone, ?action=update with acl array for share). Rust CLI sends action in the request body using the action-dispatch pattern. Also node-triton's unshare fails (exit 1) in emit-payload mode. Affects payload tests: payload-image-clone, payload-image-share, payload-image-unshare.
close_reason: Fixed across 4edc042 and 6c51b8a: image actions now use query param wire format, shared fixtures resolve all diffs

title: CLI compat: fwrule enable/disable wire format
description: node-triton sends POST /fwrules/{id}/enable with empty body. Rust CLI sends POST /fwrules/{id} with {enabled: true/false} in body. The Rust approach uses the update endpoint instead of dedicated enable/disable paths. Affects payload tests: payload-fwrule-enable, payload-fwrule-disable.

title: CLI compat: positional arg mismatches (image export, disk add, snapshot create)
description: node-triton uses positional args: 'image export UUID MANTA_PATH', 'disk add INST SIZE', 'snapshot create INST -n NAME'. Rust CLI uses --manta-path, --size, --name flags instead. Affects payload tests: payload-image-export, payload-disk-add, payload-snap-create.

title: CLI compat: support field=value positional syntax for update commands
description: node-triton uses 'image update UUID name=new-name version=2.0.0', 'fwrule update UUID rule=...', and 'vlan update ID name=...' with positional field=value pairs. Rust CLI uses named --flags instead. Affects payload tests: payload-image-update, payload-fwrule-update, payload-vlan-update.

title: No serde tests for Package struct with DiskSize untagged enum
description: The Package struct in apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/misc.rs has tricky DiskSize untagged enum (u64 or remaining string), cross-crate Brand, flexible_disk rename override, and role-tag hyphenated field. CLI tests validate fixture JSON but there is no dedicated serde test like other major types (Machine, Image, Volume, Network).

title: NIC list filter uses string comparison for state instead of typed enum
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/nic.rs:155
Line: `"state" => nic.state == value,`

**Problem:**
The NIC list filter converts NicState to a String via enum_to_display() at line 120, then compares the stringified state against the user-provided filter value at line 155. This bypasses type safety:
- An invalid state value (typo, wrong case like 'Running' vs 'running') silently filters out all results without error
- No compile-time guarantee that filter values stay in sync with NicState variants

**Other list commands do this correctly:**
- instance/list.rs uses parse_serde_enum() to parse the filter string into a typed MachineState
- volume.rs uses parse_serde_enum() to parse into typed VolumeState/VolumeType
- image.rs uses parse_serde_enum() to parse into typed ImageState/ImageType

**Suggested fix:**
Parse the user-provided state value into a NicState enum before comparing:
```rust
// In the filter section, parse 'value' into NicState first:
"state" => {
    let target: NicState = parse_serde_enum(value)
        .map_err(|_| anyhow!("Invalid state: {}", value))?;
    enum_to_display(&nic.state) == enum_to_display(&target)
}
```
Or better: apply filters on the typed Nic struct before converting to NicOutput.

**Verification:** After fix, `triton inst nic list <id> state=INVALID` should return an error instead of silently returning zero results.

title: Missing forward-compat tests for 11 enums with #[serde(other)]
description: 11 enums have #[serde(other)] Unknown but no test verifying unknown values deserialize gracefully. If the attribute were removed, no test would catch the regression. Missing: MachineType, SnapshotState (cloudapi), DiskState, ChangefeedResource, ChangefeedSubResource, MigrationState (cloudapi misc), MigrationPhase (cloudapi misc), VmAction (vmapi), SnapshotState (vmapi). 13 other enums DO have these tests.

title: Debug format in UnexpectedRecovery error variant reaches users
description: In clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:1031, the UnexpectedRecovery error uses {state:?} Debug format which can reach end users, violating Type Safety Rule 7. Should use enum_to_display or Display format instead of Debug.

title: VMAPI MigrationAction lacks #[serde(other)] Unknown variant
description: In apis/vmapi-api/src/types/vms.rs:333-344, MigrationAction has no #[serde(other)] variant. Unlike VmAction (which has Unknown), this would cause deserialization failure if VMAPI adds a new migration sub-action. Add Unknown variant for forward compatibility.

title: Duplicate MigrationPhase and SnapshotState enums across cloudapi-api and vmapi-api
description: MigrationPhase (cloudapi misc.rs:176, vmapi migrations.rs:89) and SnapshotState (cloudapi machine_resources.rs:62, vmapi vms.rs:424) have identical variants in both crates. Should use cross-crate import pattern (like Brand) to prevent drift. MigrationState and MigrationAction correctly differ and should stay separate.

title: Image sort silently ignores unknown sort fields
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs:521, _ => {} silently does nothing for typos like --sort-field=naem. Should return an error or emit a warning listing valid sort fields.

title: VLAN filter silently no-ops on unparseable vlan_id value
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/vlan.rs:131-136, vlan_id=abc silently passes all VLANs through instead of erroring. The parse failure makes the filter a no-op. Should parse outside the filter closure and return an error for invalid values.

title: NIC netmask_to_cidr silently skips unparseable octets
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/nic.rs:127-133, filter_map with .ok() silently skips unparseable netmask octets, producing wrong CIDR values. For 255.abc.255.0, it computes CIDR from only 3 octets. Should validate exactly 4 valid octets or return Option<u8> and display ? on failure.

title: Network.fabric and resolvers missing skip_serializing_if consistency
description:
apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/network.rs:78 — Network.fabric uses #[serde(default)] but no skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none", unlike all other optional fields in the codebase. This means serializing produces "fabric": null instead of omitting the field. Same issue with Network.resolvers at line 108.

Fix: Add skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none" to both fields for consistency with the rest of the codebase.

title: Untyped String fields that should be enums: volume_type, VolumeMount.mode, ImageRequirements.brand
description:
Several fields use raw String where typed enums would provide type safety:

1. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs:50 — Volume.volume_type is String. Known type is 'tritonnfs'. Should be a VolumeType enum with Tritonnfs + #[serde(other)] Unknown.

2. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs:234 — VolumeMount.mode is Option<String>. Values are 'rw' or 'ro'. Should be a MountMode enum.

3. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs:76 — ImageRequirements.brand is Option<String>. Should be Option<VmapiBrand> since it maps directly to the Brand enum.

Fix: Create typed enums for each, following the existing #[serde(other)] Unknown pattern.

title: No tests for add_auth_headers (RBAC role injection, X-Act-As, Accept-Version)
description:
clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/auth.rs — The add_auth_headers function handles RBAC role injection into query parameters (before signing, to prevent parameter tampering), X-Act-As header for operator masquerading, and Accept-Version header. None of these code paths have any tests.

If the RBAC as-role query parameter construction breaks (e.g. roles not URL-encoded, appended after signing), an attacker could tamper with role parameters. If X-Act-As or Accept-Version header injection regresses, operators lose masquerading capability silently.

Fix: Create tests that construct reqwest::Request objects, call add_auth_headers with various AuthConfig settings, and verify the resulting headers and URL query parameters.

title: Missing serde tests for User/Role/Policy, machine_resources, and migration types
description:
Several type modules in apis/cloudapi-api lack serde round-trip tests:

1. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/user.rs — User, Role, and Policy all use #[serde(rename = "role-tag")] on their role_tag fields. Given how many bugs this pattern has caused elsewhere, this is a significant gap.

2. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine_resources.rs — Snapshot, Disk, SnapshotState, DiskState have no serde tests. The #[serde(other)] Unknown forward compatibility is never verified.

3. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/misc.rs — Migration, MigrationState, MigrationPhase have no serde tests. The Migration.vm_uuid field uses #[serde(alias = "machine")] which is a tricky alias pattern.

Fix: Add serde_user.rs, serde_machine_resources.rs, and serde_migration.rs test files with fixture JSON, following the existing pattern in serde_machine.rs.

title: client-generator check hides I/O errors as STALE with unwrap_or_default()
description:
client-generator/src/main.rs:233 — The cmd_check function uses unwrap_or_default() when reading the actual generated file. If the file exists but cannot be read (permission denied, I/O error), the empty string is compared against the expected code, always producing a misleading 'STALE' result. Developer re-runs make clients-generate, code regenerates identically, CI still fails.

Fix: Handle missing-file explicitly with Err(NotFound) => String::new(), and propagate other I/O errors with an explicit error message.

title: enum_to_display silently falls back to Debug format
description:
cli/triton-cli/src/output/mod.rs:30-35 — The enum_to_display function uses .ok() on serde_json::to_value() and falls back to {:?} Debug format. This violates the project's own Type Safety Rule 7 (no Debug format for user-facing output). If serialization fails, users see MachineState::Running instead of 'running'.

Fix: Add tracing::warn when falling back to Debug format, since this should never happen for correctly-defined serde enums.

title: scan_ssh_dir_for_key silently swallows key load errors
description:
libs/triton-auth/src/key_loader.rs:259-273 — The let-chain that scans ~/.ssh/ for matching keys silently discards errors from try_exists, load_legacy_from_file, and public_key_blob. If a key file exists but fails to load (permission denied, corrupt PEM, encrypted without passphrase), the function silently moves on. User sees 'No key with fingerprint ... found' with no hint that the key was found but could not be loaded.

Fix: Restructure the let-chain into explicit match arms with tracing::debug logging for each failure reason.

title: Fingerprint::matches returns false on key serialization failure
description:
libs/triton-auth/src/fingerprint.rs:89-93 — The matches() method catches PublicKey::to_bytes() errors and returns false. If a key fails to serialize (corrupted or unsupported type like certificate keys), the method silently reports 'no match' instead of surfacing the error. User sees 'Key not found' instead of the real serialization error, misdirecting debugging effort.

Fix: Add tracing::warn on the Err branch: tracing::warn!("Failed to serialize public key for fingerprint matching: {}", e)

title: transform_create_machine_body silently no-ops on body parse failure
description:
clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:248-256 — The pre-hook that flattens tags/metadata for create_machine silently returns if the body is missing, non-bytes, or non-JSON. If Progenitor changes its body format (e.g. streaming instead of buffered), as_bytes() returns None and the body transformation silently stops. User's tags and metadata are lost with zero indication.

Fix: Add tracing::warn on early returns in transform_create_machine_body. A missing body or non-JSON body on a create_machine call is always suspicious.

title: VmState::FromStr rejects unknown states inconsistently with serde(other)
description: apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs:117-136 — VmState has #[serde(other)] Unknown for forward compatibility, but the FromStr implementation returns Err for unknown state strings instead of Ok(VmState::Unknown). This means .parse::<VmState>() and serde_json::from_str() behave differently for the same unknown input. Fix: either map unknown strings to Ok(VmState::Unknown) in FromStr to be consistent, or document that FromStr is intentionally stricter.

title: AccessKeyStatus enum may need serde rename_all attribute
description: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/key.rs:71-78 — AccessKeyStatus enum (Active, Inactive, Expired, Unknown) has no #[serde(rename_all = ...)] attribute. Variants serialize as PascalCase by default ('Active', 'Inactive', 'Expired'). The adjacent CredentialType enum at line 83 does have #[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]. If the CloudAPI wire format uses lowercase for access key status, deserialization will silently fail to match and fall through to Unknown. Fix: verify the actual wire format against the Node.js CloudAPI and add the appropriate rename_all attribute.
close_reason: Verified against Node.js source: wire format is PascalCase, current enum is correct. Added doc comment and serde tests.

title: Stringly-typed fields where typed enums exist
description:
Three locations use String fields where corresponding typed enums are defined in the same codebase:

1. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/misc.rs:250-268 — MigrationProgressEntry.phase and .state are String, but MigrationPhase and MigrationState enums exist in the same file (lines 156-182). Both enums have #[serde(other)] Unknown for forward compatibility.

2. apis/vmapi-api/src/types/jobs.rs:45 — ListJobsQuery.execution is Option<String> but JobExecution enum exists at line 63 with #[serde(other)] Unknown.

3. apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/changefeed.rs:167 — ChangefeedMessage.changed_resource_id is String but these are always VM UUIDs and should be typed as Uuid.

Fix: Replace String fields with their typed enum/Uuid equivalents.

title: config_dir() silently falls back to CWD when HOME is unset
description: cli/triton-cli/src/config/paths.rs:32-34 — dirs::home_dir().unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from(".")).join(".triton") falls back to the current working directory when HOME cannot be determined. Profiles created in one directory won't be visible from another. Configuration could leak into source repositories. Fix: emit a warning to stderr when falling back, or return an error if HOME is unset.

title: Instance list silently drops image list API failures
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/list.rs:318 — 'Err(_) => HashMap::new()' silently discards all errors from the images API call. If authentication is broken or the API is down, the image names column in instance list output will simply be blank with no indication of the failure. Fix: emit a warning to stderr: eprintln\!("warning: failed to fetch images: {}", e)

title: SSH command silently falls back to root user on any API error
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/ssh.rs:251 — fetch_image_default_user catches Err(_) for ALL API errors (auth failures, network errors, 500s) and silently returns 'root'. Users expecting a specific default user (e.g., 'ubuntu') get silently connected as root, which may then fail with 'Permission denied' and no hint that the real issue was an API failure. Fix: add tracing::debug logging of the API error before falling back.

title: Image cache save_list silently drops serialization failures
description: cli/triton-cli/src/cache.rs:52-57 — serde_json::to_string(images).unwrap_or_default() converts serialization failures into empty strings written to the cache file. If an Image type has a serde bug, the cache perpetually fails with no diagnostic. Also, 'let _ = tokio::fs::write(...)' silently drops file-write errors. Fix: use tracing::debug logging instead of unwrap_or_default(), and log write errors. The module is intentionally silent (matching node-triton), but debug-level logging would help troubleshooting.

title: Certificate loading silently drops individual certs in root store
description: cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs:326-327, 362-363, 380-381 — 'let _ = root_store.add(cert)' silently ignores errors when adding certificates to the TLS root store. If a user's CA bundle contains a malformed cert, it is silently skipped. Later TLS failures give no hint that a cert failed to load. Fix: count and log skipped certificates at tracing::debug level.

title: Silent fallbacks in triton-auth discard error context
description:
Three locations in triton-auth silently discard errors, making debugging difficult:

1. libs/triton-auth/src/lib.rs:218-230 — KeySource::Auto catches Err(_) from SSH agent and falls back to file keys. Users with agent problems get misleading 'key not found in ~/.ssh/' errors. Should distinguish KeyNotFound (expected) from other agent errors (unexpected).

2. libs/triton-auth/src/agent.rs:82-86 — list_agent_keys_sync uses 'if let Ok(pub_key) = parse_public_key_from_bytes(...)' to silently skip unparseable keys. Keys using unsupported algorithms vanish from listings with no indication.

3. libs/triton-auth/src/agent.rs:92-96 — is_agent_available() uses .unwrap_or(false) on spawn_blocking result, swallowing task panics as 'agent not available'.

Fix: Add tracing::debug logging before each fallback so --verbose reveals the real cause. For #1, also distinguish error types.

title: triton env missing Docker environment variables
description:
## Summary

When docker-setup has been run, Node.js `triton env` outputs Docker vars:

    # docker
    export DOCKER_CERT_PATH=/Users/user/.triton/docker/...
    export DOCKER_HOST=tcp://us-central-1.docker.mnx.io:2376
    export DOCKER_TLS_VERIFY=1
    export COMPOSE_HTTP_TIMEOUT=300

Rust always outputs an empty Docker section:

    # docker

This breaks the Docker setup workflow via `eval "$(triton env)"`.

## Fix

Check for docker cert/host configuration in the profile's docker setup
directory and emit the appropriate environment variables when present.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs`

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_env.rs` (create if needed)
- Create a fake docker setup directory with certs
- Assert `triton env` outputs DOCKER_* variables

Differences.md issue #9.

title: profile set-current fails for env profile
description:
## Summary

Node.js: `triton profile set-current env` → `"env" is already the current profile`
Rust: `triton profile set-current env` → `Error: Failed to read profile 'env': No such file or directory`

The "env" profile is constructed from environment variables (TRITON_URL, etc.)
and has no file in `~/.triton/profiles.d/`. The Rust `set_current_profile()`
calls `Profile::load(name)` which tries to read from disk and fails.

## Fix

`set_current_profile()` should use `resolve_profile(Some(name))` instead of
`Profile::load(name)` to handle the "env" profile, or special-case "env"
when the relevant environment variables are set.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/profile.rs` — `set_current_profile()` (around line 487-505)

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_profiles.rs`
- Set TRITON_URL/TRITON_ACCOUNT/TRITON_KEY_ID env vars
- Assert `triton profile set-current env` succeeds

Differences.md issue #4.

title: profile set alias missing (should alias set-current)
description:
## Summary

Node.js `triton profile set NAME` works as an alias for `set-current`.
Rust returns `error: unrecognized subcommand 'set'` (exit code 2).

This is a muscle-memory breaker for existing triton users.

## Fix

Add `#[command(alias = "set")]` to the `SetCurrent` variant in the profile
subcommand enum.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/profile.rs` — the `SetCurrent` enum variant
  (around line 91-95)

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_profiles.rs`
- Assert `triton profile set <name>` succeeds and behaves like `set-current`

Differences.md issue #3.

title: Double Error prefix in all API error messages
description:
## Summary

All API error messages show "Error: Error: ..." due to double-wrapping.

Node.js: `triton: error (SomeCode): message`
Rust: `Error: Error: Key not found: No key with fingerprint ...`

## Root cause

Progenitor's `Error::Custom` variant's `Display` impl adds an `"Error: "` prefix
(`write!(f, "Error: {}", s)`). Then anyhow adds another `"Error: "` when `main()`
returns `Err`.

## Fix

Either strip the `Error::Custom` wrapping before returning from main, or use a
custom error display that doesn't double-prefix. Could also consider matching
Node.js format: `triton: error (Code): message`.

## Files
- CLI main error handling (likely `cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs`)
- Wherever progenitor errors are wrapped/displayed

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_api_errors.rs` or `cli_error_paths.rs`
- Assert error messages don't contain "Error: Error:"

Differences.md issue #2.

title: Add positional key=value filter support to volume list
description:
The node-triton CLI supports positional key=value filter arguments for `triton volume list` (e.g., `triton volumes name=mydata`). The Rust CLI currently has no filter support at all (no --flag filters either).

**Reference implementation**: See `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/list.rs` (commit 3c7c005) which added this for instance list. Follow the same pattern:
1. Add --flag filter fields to `VolumeListArgs` for each supported filter
2. Add a `filters: Vec<String>` field with `#[arg(trailing_var_arg = true)]`
3. Add an `apply_positional_filters()` function that parses key=value pairs and merges into args fields
4. Validate keys against a `VALID_FILTERS` list
5. Apply filters to the API request (check what the ListVolumes API endpoint supports as query params)

**Valid filter keys** (from node-triton `lib/do_volume/do_list.js`): name, size, state, owner, type

**Note**: node-triton uses `jsonPredFromKv()` for volume filtering (server-side JSON predicate). The Rust implementation should check whether the CloudAPI ListVolumes endpoint supports query parameter filtering directly.

**File to modify**: `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/volume.rs`
**Existing flag filters**: None (only has TableFormatArgs for output formatting)

title: Add positional key=value filter support to network list
description:
The node-triton CLI supports positional key=value filter arguments for `triton network list` (e.g., `triton networks public=true`). The Rust CLI currently only supports --flag style filters.

**Reference implementation**: See `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/list.rs` (commit 3c7c005) which added this for instance list. Follow the same pattern:
1. Add a `filters: Vec<String>` field with `#[arg(trailing_var_arg = true)]` to `NetworkListArgs`
2. Add an `apply_positional_filters()` function that parses key=value pairs and merges into existing args fields
3. Validate keys against a `VALID_FILTERS` list

**Valid filter keys** (from node-triton `lib/do_network/do_list.js`): public

**Note**: This is a small change since there is only one filter key. The filtering is client-side in node-triton (filters after fetching all networks).

**File to modify**: `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/network.rs`
**Existing flag filters**: --public (bool)

title: Add positional key=value filter support to package list
description:
The node-triton CLI supports positional key=value filter arguments for `triton package list` (e.g., `triton packages memory=1024`). The Rust CLI currently only supports --flag style filters.

**Reference implementation**: See `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/list.rs` (commit 3c7c005) which added this for instance list. Follow the same pattern:
1. Add a `filters: Vec<String>` field with `#[arg(trailing_var_arg = true)]` to `PackageListArgs`
2. Add an `apply_positional_filters()` function that parses key=value pairs and merges into existing args fields
3. Use `.parse()` for numeric values (memory, disk, swap, lwps, vcpus)
4. Validate keys against a `VALID_FILTERS` list

**Valid filter keys** (from node-triton `lib/do_package/do_list.js`): name, memory, disk, swap, lwps, version, vcpus, group

**File to modify**: `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/package.rs`
**Existing flag filters**: --name, --memory, --disk (note: some node-triton filters like swap, lwps, vcpus, group may need new --flag fields too)

title: Add positional key=value filter support to image list
description:
The node-triton CLI supports positional key=value filter arguments for `triton image list` (e.g., `triton images name=base-64`). The Rust CLI currently only supports --flag style filters.

**Reference implementation**: See `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/list.rs` (commit 3c7c005) which added this for instance list. Follow the same pattern:
1. Add a `filters: Vec<String>` field with `#[arg(trailing_var_arg = true)]` to `ImageListArgs`
2. Add an `apply_positional_filters()` function that parses key=value pairs and merges into existing args fields
3. Use `parse_serde_enum()` helper for enum values (state, type), `.parse()` for primitives
4. Validate keys against a `VALID_FILTERS` list

**Valid filter keys** (from node-triton `lib/do_image/do_list.js`): name, os, version, public, state, owner, type

**File to modify**: `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs`
**Existing flag filters**: --name, --version, --os, --state (ImageState), --type/--image-type (ImageType), --public, --all

Add tests that verify JSON output fields for key commands (instance list, image list, etc.) to catch serde rename mismatches and missing fields. Could use offline fixtures or API integration.

At minimum, add help/existence tests in cli_subcommands.rs matching the pattern used for every other command group.

title: certgen filter_map silently drops malformed fingerprint hex
description: libs/triton-auth/src/certgen.rs:227-235 - md5_fingerprint_base64 uses filter_map which silently drops hex parts that fail to parse. Produces incorrect (shorter) base64 encoding for certificate issuer DN. Should use map + collect::<Result> and propagate error.

title: cloudapi command ignores --insecure TLS setting
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/cloudapi.rs:123 - Creates reqwest::Client::new() without danger_accept_invalid_certs, ignoring the user's --insecure flag. Breaks debugging against self-signed cert environments.

title: metadata set --wait hardcodes MachineState::Running
description: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/metadata.rs:228-236 - If metadata is set on a stopped instance, --wait times out because it always waits for Running. The tag set command already implements the correct pattern: capture pre-operation state and wait to return to that state.

title: Silent RSA fallback for unknown SSH key algorithms
description: libs/triton-auth/src/signature.rs:47,50,64,67 - Unknown algorithm types silently map to KeyType::Rsa, producing incorrect HTTP Signature headers. Unknown ECDSA curves also silently default to P-256. Should return an error or log a warning.

title: ChangefeedResource missing #[serde(other)] Unknown
description: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/changefeed.rs:51 - Forward compatibility violation (AGENTS.md Rule 5). If server adds a new resource type, changefeed deserialization fails. Sibling ChangefeedSubResource already has this correctly.

title: update_access_key returns HTTP 201 instead of 200
description: apis/cloudapi-api/src/lib.rs:1707,1790 - update_access_key and update_user_access_key use HttpResponseCreated (201) instead of HttpResponseOk (200). Update operations should return 200. All other update endpoints correctly use HttpResponseOk.

title: Divergent profile resolution logic between config/mod.rs and main.rs
description: cli/triton-cli/src/config/mod.rs and cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs — Profile resolution logic is duplicated between these two files with subtle differences. This can lead to different profiles being selected depending on code path. Fix: consolidate into a single resolution function.

title: CreateMachine legacy format transformation is 152 lines of manual code
description:
File: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs, lines 1109-1260

**Problem:** Node.js CloudAPI expects tags/metadata as flattened `tag.KEY=VALUE` and `metadata.KEY=VALUE` fields, but Progenitor generates nested object structures. 152 lines of manual transformation code converts between formats, including:
- `transform_progenitor_request_to_legacy()` — extracts nested fields and flattens
- `transform_create_machine_to_legacy()` — manual field-by-field construction
- Two separate methods: `create_machine()` and `create_machine_from_progenitor()`

**Suggested fix:** Investigate Progenitor `with_conversion()` for custom serialization, or implement serde custom serializer on the request type to reduce manual code.

**Verification:** Integration tests against real CloudAPI to confirm tag/metadata round-trip.
close_reason: Removed unused create_machine (API type variant) and 110-line transform_create_machine_to_legacy; renamed create_machine_from_progenitor to create_machine

title: Untyped disk state field — Option<String> should be DiskState enum
description:
CLI usage: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/disk.rs, line 143

**Current code:**
- `disk.state.as_deref().unwrap_or("unknown")` — raw string with hardcoded fallback

**Problem:** Disk state is `Option<String>` in the Progenitor-generated types but has known finite values. The CLI falls back to a hardcoded `"unknown"` string.

**Suggested fix:** Create a `DiskState` enum in cloudapi-api types (or reuse `SnapshotState` if the values overlap), then update the Disk struct to use it.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Added DiskState enum to replace Option<String>

title: Untyped NIC state field — Option<String> should be NicState enum
description:
File: apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/network.rs, line 228
CLI usage: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/nic.rs, line 155

**Current code:**
- API: `pub state: Option<String>` in Nic struct
- CLI: `"state" => nic.state == value` — raw string comparison

**Problem:** NIC state has known finite values (provisioning, running, stopped) but is modeled as `Option<String>`, losing type safety. The CLI does unvalidated string comparisons.

**Suggested fix:** Create a `NicState` enum in cloudapi-api types:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum NicState {
    Provisioning,
    Running,
    Stopped,
    #[serde(other)]
    Unknown,
}
```
Then change `Nic.state` to `Option<NicState>`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make openapi-generate && make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Added NicState enum, updated Nic.state field type
