title: manta-storage: vendor s3_suite.py from manta-ng as bench/ E2E harness
description:
Port (or vendor) the s3_suite.py harness from manta-ng into
manta-storage's bench layer so the vnext build has its own
end-to-end S3 compat suite without a manta-ng dependency.

WHY:
  - manta-ng's bench/s3_suite.py (~7700 lines, 195 scenarios) is the
    closest thing to a Ceph-s3-tests rig we have today. It surfaced
    several real gaps that the manta-buckets-api .sh scripts and our
    crate-side integration tests miss (x-amz-tagging header, MPU
    10001-part reject, ListParts pagination, conditional CopyObject
    timestamps).
  - manta-ng is the LEGACY workspace; relying on it cross-repo
    means vnext-only contributors can't run the harness without
    cloning manta-ng + provisioning bench prereqs.
  - The crate-side rust integration tests (workspace_gate.rs,
    scoped_keys_e2e.rs, etc.) only cover the in-process path; they
    can't catch end-to-end issues that need a real listener +
    real-AWS-SDK calls (signing, transport, presigned URLs, etc.).

OPTIONS:

  (a) Vendor: copy manta-ng/bench/s3_suite.py to
      manta-storage/bench/s3_suite.py verbatim, drop manta-ng-specific
      bits (CHG-NNN comments referring to manta-ng changes), and
      re-tune the default --endpoint to a manta-storage dev rig.
      Lowest effort; the suite is already self-contained Python.

  (b) Port to Rust integration crate: rewrite as
      crates/mantas3-bench-s3/ using aws-sdk-s3 + the existing
      spawn_test_server pattern from workspace_gate.rs. Higher
      effort; integrates with `cargo test --workspace`.

  (c) Hybrid: vendor (a) for now to bridge the test-coverage gap;
      port to Rust incrementally as scenarios prove durable.

Recommend (c). The Python harness is fast to iterate against a
running mantad, easy to add new tests to, and not on the critical
build path. Rust port can happen scenario-by-scenario as we hit
the same compat issues.

SCOPE:
  - vendor s3_suite.py into manta-storage/bench/ with a top-of-file
    comment marking it as a vendor copy from manta-ng SHA <X>.
  - Add a manta-storage/bench/README.md explaining when to run it
    (deploy verify) vs the crate-side tests (PR-blocking).
  - Strip the manta-ng-specific scoped-key admin URL shape
    (s3_suite uses POST /admin/v1/users/{u}/access-keys with a
    "scope" field; manta-storage uses POST .../scoped-access-keys).
    Add bearer-token support so the harness works against deployments
    with MANTAD_ADMIN_TOKEN set.
  - Skip / pre-filter known-AWS-spec divergences (the TooManyTags vs
    InvalidTag debate per t_tag_count_exceeds_10).

PREREQUISITES:
  - Python3 + boto3 on a host that can reach the mantad endpoint.
  - Optional: a manta-storage CI worker with the Python deps
    pre-installed if we want this to run as a smoke step.

EXIT CRITERIA:
  - manta-storage/bench/s3_suite.py runs against a fresh mantad
    deploy and reports the same pass/fail breakdown as today's
    run via manta-ng (~ 80+ pass, real gaps tracked as their own
    beads).
  - README.md documents the run command + expected output shape.

PRIORITY: P3 -- coverage-gap closure; functional behavior is
unchanged. Useful as a smoke harness for every mantad release.

title: mantas3-s3 CopyObject: x-amz-copy-source-if-unmodified-since not honored
description:
Found by manta-ng/bench/s3_suite.py against vnext mantad on
192.168.1.182:7443.

Failing test: s3_suite.py::t_copy_object_if_unmodified_since_412_when_modified
(line 5602).

Sequence:
  - PutObject k1 at time T0 (captures last-modified)
  - PutObject k1 again at T0 + delta (last-modified bumps)
  - CopyObject src=k1 -> dest=k2 with
    x-amz-copy-source-if-unmodified-since: <T0>
  - Expect 412 PreconditionFailed (source was modified since T0)
  - Observed: copy succeeds

AWS S3 spec: CopyObject honors four conditional headers on the
source:
  x-amz-copy-source-if-match              (ETag equality)
  x-amz-copy-source-if-none-match         (ETag inequality)
  x-amz-copy-source-if-modified-since     (timestamp gt)
  x-amz-copy-source-if-unmodified-since   (timestamp lt-or-eq)

When the precondition fails the response is 412
PreconditionFailed (no copy performed).

Mantad's CopyObject in crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/ops/copy.rs
appears to skip the conditional-header check entirely (passes the
`copy_object_if_match_*` and `copy_object_if_none_match_*` tests
elsewhere only for the etag family, not the timestamp family --
need to confirm).

FIX SHAPE:
  - In copy_object_impl, after loading the source ObjectMeta,
    evaluate all four conditional headers against
    (src_om.etag, src_om.last_modified). Reuse the
    check_get_conditional helper (already exists in
    crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/ops/object.rs::get_object_impl
    for GetObject's RFC-7232 family) if applicable, or write a
    sibling for the copy-source-* prefix headers.
  - Return s3_error!(PreconditionFailed) on mismatch (maps to 412).

TEST COVERAGE TARGETS (port from manta-ng):
  - copy_object_if_match_succeeds_when_etag_matches      (PASSES today)
  - copy_object_if_match_412_when_etag_mismatches        (PASSES today)
  - copy_object_if_none_match_412_when_etag_matches      (PASSES today)
  - copy_object_if_modified_since_succeeds_when_modified (PASSES today)
  - copy_object_if_unmodified_since_412_when_modified    (FAILS -- this fix)
  - copy_object_if_unmodified_since_succeeds_when_unmodified

Look at the etag-family tests passing already; the unmodified-since
gap may be the only timestamp branch missing.

PRIORITY: P3 -- conditional copy is a niche path; aws-cli's `aws s3 cp`
doesn't set these headers by default. Some sync tools (rclone, mc) do.

title: mantas3-s3 CompleteMultipartUpload: 10001-part overflow returns InvalidPart instead of InvalidRequest
description:
Found by manta-ng/bench/s3_suite.py against vnext mantad on
192.168.1.182:7443.

Failing test: s3_suite.py::t_mpu_complete_10001_parts_rejected_invalid_request
(line 5780).

Sequence: CompleteMultipartUpload with 10001 parts.
Expected error code: InvalidRequest
Observed: InvalidPart

AWS S3 spec caps a single multipart upload at 10,000 parts. The
CompleteMultipartUpload reject for part-count > 10000 returns
`InvalidRequest`, not `InvalidPart`. The latter is reserved for
per-part-entry failures (missing etag, gap in part numbers, etc.).

Mantad's CompleteMultipartUpload handler in
crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/ops/multipart.rs::complete_multipart_upload_impl
checks monotonic part numbers (lines 304-309) and returns
`InvalidPart` for any anomaly, including the 10001-part case.

FIX SHAPE:
  - Add a length check on requested_parts (or selected) before the
    monotonic-part-number loop:
        if requested_parts.len() > 10_000 {
            return Err(s3_error!(
                InvalidRequest,
                "multipart upload exceeds 10000 parts",
            ));
        }
  - Keep the existing InvalidPart path for the per-entry failures.

TEST COVERAGE TARGET:
  - Upload 10001 parts -> Complete returns InvalidRequest (the
    failing case).
  - Upload 10000 parts -> Complete succeeds (boundary inclusive).

PRIORITY: P3 -- requires 10001 actual part uploads to trigger; only
matters at the >5 GiB object boundary AWS clients hit. Wire-shape
correctness for AWS-SDK-driven big-uploads.

The big-size cases (5 GiB+1 single part, 10000 × 5 MiB MPU) are
SKIP-by-default in s3_suite.py because they transfer significant
bytes; this part-count check is the cheap variant.

title: mantas3-s3 ListParts: part-number-marker / max-parts not honored (returns full list)
description:
Found by manta-ng/bench/s3_suite.py against vnext mantad on
192.168.1.182:7443.

Failing test: s3_suite.py::t_list_parts_pagination_part_number_marker
(line 6790).

Test sequence:
  - Create MPU upload
  - Upload 5 parts (part_number 1..5)
  - ListParts with max_parts=2, part_number_marker=2
  - Expect parts = [3, 4]; observed parts = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

S3 spec: ListParts honors `part-number-marker` (start strictly after
the given part number) and `max-parts` (cap on returned entries).
Both are required for any client doing paged enumeration of large
multipart uploads.

Mantad currently returns the full list regardless of marker / max.
See crates/mantas3/s3/src/s3_impl/ops/multipart.rs::list_parts_impl.

FIX SHAPE:
  - Read input.part_number_marker (Option<i32>) and input.max_parts
    (Option<i32>); apply standard windowing on the
    self.meta.list_parts(...) result before mapping to Part DTOs.
  - Set output.is_truncated = true when the result is clipped, and
    output.next_part_number_marker to the last returned part number
    so the client can resume.
  - max_parts caps at 1000 (AWS default); negative or missing falls
    back to 1000.

TEST COVERAGE TARGETS (mirror manta-ng):
  - parts [3, 4] after marker=2 max=2 (the failing case)
  - parts [5] after marker=4 max=2 (no truncation, no next_marker)
  - max_parts = 1000 caps at 1000 even when 1001+ uploaded
  - is_truncated + next_part_number_marker correct shape

PRIORITY: P3 -- only matters when an upload has 1000+ parts; client
SDKs auto-paginate via these fields but our list returning the
whole set today means clients get the full list anyway. Functional
regression risk on multi-GB uploads at the >1000 part boundary.

title: vnext webui: scoped-access-key creation UI (admin-backend route + SPA form)
description:
Discovered while landing monitor-reef-971j (mantad scoped-key admin endpoint).

The published triton-admin (admin-backend BFF) doesn't surface any IAM
access-key creation today -- scoped or unscoped. Adding the scoped-key
form needs three layers:

  1. admin-backend BFF (mariana-trench/services/triton-admin/):
     proxy POST /api/users/{name}/scoped-access-keys ->
     mantad /admin/v1/users/{name}/scoped-access-keys (workspace bearer
     OR cluster-admin bearer). Mantad client method already exists
     (mantad-client::MantadClient::create_scoped_access_key).

  2. SPA frontend: "Mint scoped key" form -- user picker, repeat-add
     scope rows (bucket / level / optional key_prefix), confirmation
     modal that surfaces the cleartext secret_access_key once with a
     "save now" hint and a copy-to-clipboard button. Match the pattern
     of any existing unscoped-key UI when it lands.

  3. Republish: strip + ship the new admin-backend binary to the test
     rig per docs/vnext/admin-webui-install.md.

PREREQUISITES:
  - The currently-published admin-backend is the legacy SDC BFF
    skeleton (docs/vnext/admin-webui-install.md "Building from source"
    section). A new admin-backend crate built against tritond-client +
    mantad-client is the right shape; the published binary diverged
    from current source long ago.

PRIORITY: P3 -- functional via tcadm + curl today; webui is convenience.

REFERENCE:

title: manta-storage S3 surface: DeleteObjects with empty Object list returns MalformedXML
description:
Found during full S3 compat sweep against vnext mantad on 192.168.1.182:7443 (2026-06-03).

manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-bulk-delete-test.sh exercises
DeleteObjects with an empty Object list and expects success (mantad
should treat 0 deletions as a no-op). Currently mantad returns:

  aws: An error occurred (MalformedXML) when calling the
       DeleteObjects operation: Unknown

92 of 93 sub-tests pass; only this empty-list case fails.

AWS S3 spec actually requires at least one Object element in the
DeleteObjects body — real AWS returns 'MalformedXML' too (the XSD
sets minOccurs=1 on <Object>). So mantad's current behavior arguably
matches AWS, and the test's expectation is the one that needs to
change. BUT:
  - Some popular S3 clients (mc, rclone) tolerate empty list as no-op
  - The behavioral parity with the legacy Node.js manta-buckets-api
    matters for migration testing

DECISION SHAPE:
  (a) Accept empty list as a no-op (returns empty Deleted[]) — matches
      old manta-buckets-api, helps client tolerance, diverges from
      AWS-strict
  (b) Keep MalformedXML (matches AWS) and update the test to expect it

PRIORITY: P3 — single edge case, both real S3 and our current
behavior reject it. Document the divergence and pick a side.

REPRO:
  aws s3api delete-objects --bucket <b> --delete '{"Objects":[]}'

OBSERVED:
  HTTP 400 MalformedXML 'Unknown'

title: manta-buckets-api test: s3-compat-s3cmd-test.sh defaults to HTTPS but vnext endpoint is HTTP
description:
Found during full S3 compat sweep against vnext mantad on 192.168.1.182:7443 (2026-06-03).

The s3-compat-s3cmd-test.sh script invokes s3cmd which defaults to
HTTPS. Mantad's S3 listener on the test rig is plain HTTP, so s3cmd
panics on the TLS handshake:

  ERROR: SSL certificate verification failure: [SSL: WRONG_VERSION_NUMBER]
         wrong version number (_ssl.c:1081)

Cascades into 7 of the 11 sub-tests failing (list, get, MPU x3, get ACL,
set ACL) because they share the misconfigured s3cmd client.

This is a TEST-SIDE issue in the manta-buckets-api repo; mantad does
nothing wrong here. The fix shape is one of:
  (a) generate s3cmd config with 'use_https = False' + 'host_base = ...'
      derived from the $S3_ENDPOINT scheme, OR
  (b) pass --no-check-certificate --no-ssl --host=... explicit flags

UPSTREAM REPO: manta-buckets-api/test/test-scripts/s3-compat-s3cmd-test.sh
NO mantad-side fix required.

VERIFICATION: the 4 sub-tests that don't go through s3cmd (s3cmd put,
s3cmd delete, s3cmd ls, s3cmd info) pass — proving the underlying
endpoint is fine.

PRIORITY: P3 — test-environment hygiene, not a product gap. File as
upstream PR against the manta-buckets-api test corpus.

If two test runs execute concurrently on the same machine, they generate identical resource names, causing creation conflicts or one test operating on another's resources. title: Debug format on user-facing println: "Removed msg: {:?}" in manatee-echo-resolver
description:
File: cli/manatee-echo-resolver/src/main.rs, line 131

Current code: println!("Removed msg: {:?}", msg.0)

Uses Debug format for user-facing println output. The inner type is BackendKey which has Display.

Fix: Change to println!("Removed msg: {}", msg.0)

Verification: Build with make package-build PACKAGE=manatee-echo-resolver and confirm output uses Display format.

title: Debug format on user-facing println: "Added msg: {:?}" in manatee-echo-resolver
description:
File: cli/manatee-echo-resolver/src/main.rs, line 128

Current code: println!("Added msg: {:?}", msg.key)

Uses Debug format for user-facing println output. BackendKey derives both Debug and Display, so Display would give cleaner output (no quotes around the string).

Fix: Change to println!("Added msg: {}", msg.key)

Verification: Build with make package-build PACKAGE=manatee-echo-resolver and confirm output uses Display format.

title: Debug format anti-pattern: format!("{:?}", DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase() in manatee-echo-resolver
description:
File: cli/manatee-echo-resolver/src/main.rs, line 76

Current code: format!("{:?}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase()

This is the exact documented anti-pattern: using Debug format on an enum and then lowercasing. It relies on the Debug representation of slog::Level happening to produce the desired output.

Fix: slog::Level implements Display (which produces the lowercase string directly). Replace with:
  format!("{}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL) or util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL.to_string()

Verification: Build with make package-build PACKAGE=manatee-echo-resolver and confirm --help output still shows the correct default log level.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on RoleTagResource in role_tags.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/role_tags.rs, line 25
Uses format\!("{:?}", resource_type) as fallback in user-facing error message.

Current code:
    let type_name = resource_type
        .to_possible_value()
        .map(|v| v.get_name().to_string())
        .unwrap_or_else(|| format\!("{:?}", resource_type));

Since RoleTagResource derives ValueEnum, to_possible_value() should never return None.
The {:?} fallback would produce "Instance" instead of "instance" if triggered.

Suggested fix - simplify since ValueEnum guarantees a value:
    let type_name = resource_type
        .to_possible_value()
        .expect("ValueEnum always has a value")
        .get_name()
        .to_string();

Verification: make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli

title: TableBuilder::add_row() does not validate row length

title: No tests for GetMachineError 410 Gone recovery logic
description: TypedClient::get_machine in clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs (lines 974-1013) implements special handling for HTTP 410 Gone responses where the server returns a Machine body instead of an Error body. The recovery logic catches Error::InvalidResponsePayload, tries to parse as Machine, and validates state == Deleted. There are no unit or integration tests for any GetMachineError variant (NotFound, JsonParse, UnexpectedRecovery). If the InvalidResponsePayload variant name changes in a Progenitor update, or if the MachineState::Deleted comparison breaks, the 410 recovery would silently fail.

title: Share/unshare image tests only cover empty ACL path
description:
In cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_images.rs, the payload tests for image share and unshare only test against a fake GET response with acl: None (empty ACL):

- test_image_share_payload: only validates adding a UUID to an empty ACL, never tests the deduplication path (sharing with an account already in the ACL)
- test_image_unshare_payload: only validates unsharing from an empty ACL (which is a no-op), never tests actual removal from a populated ACL

The share_image/unshare_image methods in cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs implement non-trivial ACL logic (dedup check, retain filter). If retain logic were inverted or dedup removed, no test would catch it.

title: TableBuilder sort silently ignores unknown field names
description: In cli/triton-cli/src/output/table.rs lines 105-109, the TableBuilder::print method's sort logic silently falls through when the user-provided sort field doesn't match any header name. A user typing 'triton ls -s nme' (typo for name) gets unsorted output with no feedback. Should emit a warning to stderr: eprintln\!("warning: unknown sort field '{}', output is unsorted", field). This is related to but distinct from monitor-reef-mjl1 which covers image.rs's custom sort logic.

title: emit-payload fixture loading silently swallows file/JSON errors
description:
In clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs (debug-only code):

1. load_fixture() (lines 359-366): chains .ok()? on fs::read_to_string and serde_json::from_str. If TRITON_EMIT_PAYLOAD_FIXTURES env var points to a bad path or invalid JSON, silently falls through to hardcoded fakes with no warning.

2. fixture_response_body() (line 388): serde_json::to_string(template).ok()? silently swallows re-serialization errors.

3. emit_request_payload() (lines 287-291): non-JSON request body silently replaced with Value::Null, hiding malformed body content.

All are debug-only but cause confusing test failures. Should eprintln warnings when env var is set but file cannot be loaded/parsed.

title: GetMachineError::Client flattens all errors to String
description:
In clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs:1011, the catch-all arm of get_machine converts Progenitor errors to String via e.to_string(), discarding the error variant and source chain:

    Err(e) => Err(GetMachineError::Client(e.to_string()))

This handles CommunicationError, InvalidRequest, PreHookError, etc. Downstream code cannot distinguish network failures from auth failures from server errors. Consider wrapping the original Error<types::Error> or at minimum preserving the error variant name in the string.

title: Minor SSH silent fallbacks: port tag and HOME directory
description:
Two minor silent fallback issues in the SSH command:

1. cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/ssh.rs:130-132 — If the tritoncli.ssh.port tag contains a non-numeric value (e.g., 'abc'), .parse::<u16>().ok() silently discards the parse error and falls back to port 22. The user set a custom port tag and has no idea it's being ignored.

2. cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/ssh.rs:281 — std::env::var("HOME").unwrap_or_else(|_| "/tmp".to_string()) falls back to /tmp when HOME is unset. The ControlPath will point to /tmp/.triton/tmp/ which may have unintended side effects and doesn't match the config_dir() resolution logic.

title: No end-to-end test for CertGenerator::generate()

This module generates X.509 certificates for Docker TLS setup, a security-sensitive operation. The unit tests verify encoding primitives but do not verify that a generated certificate is actually valid or parseable by standard TLS libraries. Fix: add a test that generates a certificate with CertGenerator and verifies the result parses as valid X.509.

title: Missing serde tests for types with tricky rename attributes
description:
Several CloudAPI types with non-trivial serde configurations lack round-trip deserialization tests:

1. FirewallRule (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/firewall.rs) — has #[serde(rename = "role-tag")] hyphenated rename
2. SshKey (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/key.rs) — also has #[serde(rename = "role-tag")]
3. Package and DiskSize (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/misc.rs) — DiskSize is #[serde(untagged)] which is fragile with serde (variant ordering matters)
4. Changefeed types (apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/changefeed.rs) — multiple rename strategies (rename_all = "lowercase", rename_all = "snake_case") and the canonical #[serde(other)] pattern

The commit history shows serde rename bugs are real (multiple commits fix snake_case vs camelCase issues). Add fixture-based serde round-trip tests for these types.

title: Exit code 3 for not-found errors not implemented
description:
## Summary

Node.js triton uses exit code 3 for "resource not found" errors (documented
in help text). Rust only uses 0, 1, 2 (where 2 is clap usage errors).

This may affect scripts that check exit codes to distinguish "error" from
"not found".

## Fix

When an API call returns 404 or a resource lookup fails, exit with code 3
instead of 1. This requires catching the specific error type before it
reaches the generic anyhow error handler in main.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs` — error handling / exit code logic
- May need a custom error type or match on HTTP status codes

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_error_paths.rs`
- Assert `triton instance get nonexistent-uuid` exits with code 3
- Assert other errors still exit with code 1

Differences.md issue #16.

title: triton env missing --triton/--docker/--smartdc/--unset flags
description:
## Summary

Node.js `triton env` supports flags to control which sections are emitted:
- `-t/--triton` — emit only triton section
- `-d/--docker` — emit only docker section
- `-s/--smartdc` — emit only smartdc section
- `-u/--unset` — emit unset commands instead of export

Rust only has `-s/--shell` to choose shell type (bash/fish). All sections
are always emitted and there is no unset mode.

## Fix

Add the missing flags to the env command. The `-s` flag conflicts — Node
uses `-s` for `--smartdc` while Rust uses `-s` for `--shell`. Need to
decide on flag mapping. Suggest using `--shell` (long only) for shell
type to free up `-s` for `--smartdc` compatibility.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/env.rs`

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_env.rs` (create if needed)
- Assert `--triton` emits only the triton section
- Assert `--unset` emits `unset` commands

Differences.md issue #11.

title: Top-level aliases differ from Node.js (ls, instances, rm)
description:
## Summary

Node.js triton has top-level shortcut commands that Rust implements differently:

| Alias       | Node.js                    | Rust                        |
|-------------|----------------------------|-----------------------------|
| ls          | Shortcut for instance list | Alias on insts (works)      |
| instances   | Shortcut for instance list | Alias on insts (works)      |
| rm          | Shortcut for instance delete | Alias on delete (works)   |

The Rust versions work but are implemented as aliases on different commands,
which may cause minor behavioral differences (help text, error messages).

Additionally, Rust adds `svcs` as an alias for `services` which Node.js
doesn't have (Node returns "unknown command" for svcs). This is additive
and not a bug.

## Fix

Verify that `ls`, `instances`, and `rm` produce identical output to their
Node.js equivalents. If there are differences in behavior (not just help
text), fix them. If behavior matches, this can be closed as compatible.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/main.rs` or wherever top-level commands are defined

## Test
- Comparison framework alias tests (API tier) already cover `insts`, `imgs`,
  `pkgs`, `nets`. Could add `ls` and `rm` alias tests.

Differences.md issue #13.
close_reason: compatible: ls, instances, and rm aliases all route to the exact same handler functions (instance::list::run and instance::delete::run) with identical argument structs. The only difference is help text, which is expected for top-level shortcuts vs nested subcommands. 225 subcommand alias tests pass confirming all aliases work correctly.

title: profile set-current output message differs from Node.js
description:
## Summary

Node.js outputs contextual messages:
- `Set "X" as current profile` (when changing)
- `"X" is already the current profile` (when already set)

Rust always prints: `Current profile: X` — no distinction between
setting a new profile vs confirming the current one.

## Fix

Check if the requested profile is already current before setting.
Print the appropriate message in each case.

## File
- `cli/triton-cli/src/commands/profile.rs` — around line 503

## Test
- `cli/triton-cli/tests/cli_profiles.rs`
- Assert message is `Set "X" as current profile` when changing
- Assert message is `"X" is already the current profile` when already set

Differences.md issue #5.

Node.js 'triton image' has 'copy (cp)' but Rust only has 'copy' without the 'cp' alias.

Comparison test ID: completion-bash

title: triton env uses single quotes instead of double quotes (bash)

Rust CLI uses single quotes in bash export statements, Node.js uses double quotes.

Node.js: export TRITON_PROFILE="env"
Rust:    export TRITON_PROFILE='env'

title: triton profile list -j shows insecure instead of curr field

title: Debug format anti-pattern on slog::Level in manatee-echo-resolver
description:
File: cli/manatee-echo-resolver/src/main.rs, line 76

This is the canonical anti-pattern: `format!("{:?}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase()` — formatting an enum with Debug and calling .to_lowercase().

Code:
```rust
format!("{:?}", util::DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL).to_lowercase()
```

slog::Level is an external library type so enum_to_display() is not directly applicable. However, the pattern is fragile — Debug output format is not guaranteed stable.

Suggested fix: Use slog::Level display string directly if available, or define a const for the default level name string.

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=manatee-echo-resolver`.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on MigrationState/Phase in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, lines 1555, 1568, 1569

MigrationState and MigrationPhase enums are displayed using Debug format {:?} instead of wire-format strings.

Examples:
- Line 1555: `println!("{}: {:?} ({:?})", m.vm_uuid, m.state, m.phase);`
- Line 1568: `println!("  State: {:?}", migration.state);`
- Line 1569: `println!("  Phase: {:?}", migration.phase);`

Debug format produces "Running"/"Begin" while wire format is "running"/"begin" (serde rename_all = "lowercase").

Suggested fix:
```rust
println!("{}: {} ({})", m.vm_uuid, enum_to_display(&m.state), enum_to_display(&m.phase));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify migration output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on JobExecution in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, lines 1153, 1166, 1184, 1205

JobExecution enum is displayed using Debug format {:?} in four places instead of the wire-format string.

Examples:
- Line 1153: `println!("{}: {:?}", job.uuid, job.execution);`
- Line 1166: `println!("Execution: {:?}", job.execution);`
- Line 1184: `println!("  Execution: {:?}", job.execution);`
- Line 1205: `println!("{}: {:?}", job.uuid, job.execution);`

Debug format produces "Succeeded" while wire format is "succeeded" (serde rename_all = "lowercase").

Suggested fix:
```rust
println!("{}: {}", job.uuid, enum_to_display(&job.execution));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify job execution output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: Debug format anti-pattern on Brand in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, line 807

Brand enum is displayed using Debug format {:?} instead of the wire-format string.

Code: `println!("Brand: {:?}", vm.brand);`

Debug format produces "Bhyve" while wire format is "bhyve" (serde rename_all = "lowercase").

Suggested fix:

```rust
println!("Brand: {}", enum_to_display(&vm.brand));
```

title: Debug format anti-pattern on VmState in vmapi-cli
description:
File: cli/vmapi-cli/src/main.rs, lines 779, 808, 1525

VmState enum is displayed using Debug format {:?} instead of the wire-format string. VmState has a Display impl in apis/vmapi-api/src/types/common.rs, but the CLI should use enum_to_display() for consistency with triton-cli.

Examples:
- Line 779: `println!("{}: {} ({:?})", vm.uuid, alias_str, vm.state);`
- Line 808: `println!("State: {:?}", vm.state);`
- Line 1525: `println!("  {}: {:?}", uuid, status.state);`

Debug format produces "Running" while wire format is "running". This is inconsistent with triton-cli output.

Suggested fix: Use enum_to_display() from triton-cli output module, or add a similar helper to vmapi-cli:
```rust
println!("{}: {} ({})", vm.uuid, alias_str, enum_to_display(&vm.state));
```

Verification: Build with `make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-cli` and verify output shows lowercase wire-format strings.

title: Hardcoded "unknown" fallback strings in CLI output code
description:
Files:
- cli/cloudapi-cli/src/main.rs:288,309 — `.unwrap_or_else(|| "unknown".to_string())`
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/image.rs:946,987 — same pattern
- cli/triton-cli/src/commands/instance/disk.rs:143 — `.unwrap_or("unknown")`
- cli/triton-cli/tests/common/mod.rs:94 — same pattern

**Problem:** Multiple CLI files use hardcoded `"unknown"` as fallback for optional enum values. Inconsistent with the `Unknown` variants that already exist on state enums.

**Suggested fix:** Define `const UNKNOWN_DISPLAY: &str = "unknown"` in a shared output module (e.g., `cli/triton-cli/src/output/mod.rs`), or better yet, leverage the `Unknown` enum variants directly by making the state fields non-optional where possible.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli && make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-cli`
close_reason: Added opt_enum_to_display helper and UNKNOWN_DISPLAY constant

title: Action dispatch enums missing Clone+Serialize derives
description:
Files:
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine.rs — MachineAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs — ImageAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/volume.rs — VolumeAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/machine_resources.rs — DiskAction: `Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema`
- apis/vmapi-api (VmAction): `Debug, Clone, Deserialize, JsonSchema` — missing Serialize

**Problem:** Action dispatch enums lack `Clone` and `Serialize` derives. While these are only used in request body deserialization (not CLI-facing), missing derives reduce testability and round-trip capability.

**Suggested fix:** Add `Clone, Serialize` to all action dispatch enums for consistency with other enum types.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=cloudapi-api && make package-build PACKAGE=vmapi-api`
close_reason: Added Clone+Serialize derives to all action dispatch enums

title: HTTP 410 Gone handling bypasses Progenitor client with 70 lines of manual code
description:
File: clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs, lines 551-620

**Problem:** CloudAPI returns HTTP 410 Gone with a Machine body (not an error) for deleted machines. Progenitor treats 410 as an error and fails to parse. Workaround: 70 lines of manual HTTP handling that bypasses Progenitor entirely, including:
- Manual `get_machine()` method with direct reqwest usage
- Custom `GetMachineError` enum with `NotFound` variant
- Bypasses Progenitor response parsing

**Suggested fix:** Investigate Progenitor response override capabilities. May require upstream feature request for non-error 4xx body handling.

**Verification:** `make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli` — deleted machine queries should still work.
close_reason: Fixed: wrap Progenitor call, catch InvalidResponsePayload for 410 recovery

comments.author: Nahum Shalman
comments.text: Fixed by wrapping Progenitor get_machine() and catching InvalidResponsePayload (which surfaces when 410 Gone body can't be deserialized as Error). Reduced from 70 lines of manual HTTP/auth code to a 24-line match on the Progenitor error. Also removed cloudapi-cli (dead code, superseded by triton-cli) and switched all CLI callers to use the typed wrapper.

title: Hardcoded enum string "failed" in rbac/apply.rs
description:
File: cli/triton-cli/src/commands/rbac/apply.rs, line 797

**Current code:**
- `status: "failed".to_string()` in `ApplyChangeResult`

**Problem:** The `status` field in `ApplyChangeResult` is a `String` that takes values "ok", "failed", "skipped". These should be a typed enum.

**Suggested fix:** Define an `ApplyStatus` enum:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum ApplyStatus {
    Ok,
    Failed,
    Skipped,
}
```
Then change `ApplyChangeResult.status` from `String` to `ApplyStatus`.

**Verification:** `make package-build PACKAGE=triton-cli && make package-test PACKAGE=triton-cli`
close_reason: Replaced hardcoded status strings with ApplyStatus enum

title: .gitignore *.tar.gz pattern is repo-wide
description: Comment says eng buildimage targets but *.tar.gz matches any tarball anywhere in repo. Consider scoping to images/**/*.tar.gz or updating comment to note broad scope.

title: Design doc TODO references local filesystem path
description: TODO in zone-image-builds.md references ~/Workspace/jenkins-joylib/... which is one developers local path. Remove or replace with PR/commit reference once pushed.

title: ExportImageRequest has redundant rename_all = camelCase
description: In apis/cloudapi-api/src/types/image.rs:263, ExportImageRequest has #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] at the struct level, but its only field (manta_path) has an explicit #[serde(rename = "manta_path")] override. The struct-level attribute is harmless but misleading -- a reader might think the wire format is camelCase when it's actually snake_case. Consider removing the struct-level rename_all since every field already has an explicit rename.

title: Stale and misleading comments in cloudapi-client lib.rs
description:
Several comments in clients/internal/cloudapi-client/src/lib.rs are inaccurate or stale:

1. Line 1596: Section header says 'Infallible serialization helper' but to_json_value panics on failure. In Rust, 'infallible' means cannot fail. Should say 'Serialization helper'.

2. Lines 298-308: emit_and_fake_get_response doc says 'returns {id: lastSeg, name: lastSeg} for single-resource GETs' but the function now dispatches to typed fake response builders via fake_response_body. The {id, name} pattern is only the final fallback for unknown operations.

3. Lines 781-787: TypedClient doc says it provides methods for 'machines, images, volumes, disks' but omits RBAC operations, create_machine, list_machines_with_tags, and get_machine (with 410 handling).

title: JSON key ordering: Rust alphabetical vs Node.js insertion order
description: Comparison test IDs: image-get, network-get, instance-get. After fixing 4-space indent (bg6), these tests still DIFF due to key ordering. Rust Progenitor-generated structs serialize fields alphabetically, while Node.js preserves API response order. Fixing requires either serde_json preserve_order feature + raw HTTP response bodies, or normalizing key order in the comparison script.
